But we still call it a building.

Meaning, a thing that is in the process of being built.

Except it isn't, because it's already done.

If it were still being built, we couldn't go inside.

The fact that we can go inside means it isn't being built anymore.

So every time you walk into a building, you walk into a thing whose name doesn't match its reality.

And nobody finds this strange.
en.editorNote: Send this to a coworker zoning out in the elevator after it hits you on the way into the office, and see if they can keep working normally
variations: A building has been built, but we still call it a building. Calling it a 'built' would be more accurate, but nobody does. Language beats logic.

items.sourceNote: 麵包掉到地上等於把地球做成三明治的 shower thought，改寫為廢話文學

zh-tw.content:
你手上拿著一片吐司。

吐司不小心掉到地上。

從你的角度看，這是吐司掉了。

從地球的角度看，這是吐司貼在地球上。

如果你再從另一個方向掉一片下去，地球的兩面就都有吐司了。

地球本身，就被夾在兩片吐司中間。

結論：你只要掉兩次吐司，就把地球做成了一個三明治。

而且這個三明治，沒有人能吃掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在公司茶水間吐司掉地上的時候傳給同事，順便宣告你剛剛把整個地球升級成早餐
variations: 麵包掉地上叫做浪費食物。麵包掉地上的另一個說法，叫做幫地球加料。

en.content:
You're holding a slice of toast.

The toast accidentally falls on the floor.

From your perspective, the toast dropped.

From Earth's perspective, the toast is now stuck to it.

If you drop another slice from the other side, both sides of the planet have toast on them.

Earth itself is now sandwiched between two slices of bread.

Conclusion: dropping toast twice turns the planet into a sandwich.

And nobody can eat that sandwich.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker after dropping toast in the office kitchen — declare that you just upgraded the entire planet into breakfast
variations: Dropping bread on the floor is called wasting food. Dropping bread on the floor is also called adding a topping to Earth.

items.sourceNote: 肥皂掉地上是地板乾淨還是肥皂變髒的 shower thought，改寫為廢話文學

zh-tw.content:
肥皂的功能，是把髒東西變乾淨。

地板上有髒東西。

你把肥皂掉到地板上。

根據肥皂的功能，地板現在應該變乾淨了。

但根據常識，肥皂現在變髒了。

所以肥皂同時讓地板變乾淨，又讓自己變髒。

這代表肥皂是用犧牲自己的方式，去清潔別人。

而你接下來要做的事，就是把這個犧牲了自己的肥皂，再拿去洗自己。

結論：你每天都在用一個受傷的東西照顧自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 洗澡洗到一半肥皂掉下去的時候傳給住在一起的家人，順便為剛剛踩到的肥皂默哀三秒
variations: 肥皂掉到地上之後，到底是肥皂變髒了，還是地板變乾淨了？答案是兩個都對，因為沒有人想知道答案。

en.content:
The job of soap is to make dirty things clean.

The floor has dirty things on it.

You drop the soap onto the floor.

By soap logic, the floor should now be clean.

By common sense, the soap is now dirty.

So the soap simultaneously cleans the floor and ruins itself.

This means soap cleans others by sacrificing itself.

And the next thing you'll do is take that self-sacrificed soap and use it to wash yourself.

Conclusion: every day, you take care of yourself using something that's already wounded.
en.editorNote: Send to a family member you live with after dropping the soap mid-shower — observe a three-second silence for the bar you just stepped on
variations: When soap falls on the floor, did the soap get dirty or did the floor get clean? Both answers are correct, because nobody wants to find out.

items.sourceNote: 鬧鐘鈴聲是你的開機主題曲 shower thought，改寫為廢話文學

zh-tw.content:
你每天早上都被同一個聲音吵醒。

那個聲音，是你自己選的。

選的時候，你覺得它好聽。

但聽了一個禮拜之後，你開始討厭它。

聽了一個月之後，你聽到任何地方播這個聲音，都會直接清醒。

聽了一年之後，你已經沒辦法回頭喜歡這首歌了。

結論：鬧鐘鈴聲，是你親手挑選來毀掉自己人生中一首歌的功能。

而你每年都會再挑一首新的來毀掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在 Spotify 隨機播放結果跳到你的舊鬧鐘鈴聲、嚇到差點摔手機的時候傳給朋友，順便警告他選鬧鐘要慎重
variations: 選鬧鐘鈴聲，本質上是在挑一首你決定要毀掉的歌。挑得越喜歡，毀得越徹底。

en.content:
Every morning, the same sound wakes you up.

You picked that sound yourself.

When you picked it, you thought it sounded nice.

After a week, you started hating it.

After a month, hearing it anywhere snaps you wide awake instantly.

After a year, you can no longer go back to enjoying that song.

Conclusion: an alarm tone is a feature you use to personally destroy one song from your life.

And every year, you pick a brand new one to destroy.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend after Spotify shuffle plays your old alarm tone and you almost throw your phone — warn them to choose their next ringtone carefully
variations: Picking an alarm tone is essentially choosing which song you'd like to ruin. The more you like it, the more thoroughly it gets ruined.

items.sourceNote: 我們躲雨回家然後站在淋浴下的 shower thought，改寫為廢話文學

zh-tw.content:
下雨的時候，你會撐傘，會躲雨，會抱怨衣服濕了。

回到家之後，你做的第一件事，是進浴室。

進浴室之後，你打開蓮蓬頭。

蓮蓬頭噴出來的東西，本質上就是雨。

你站在底下，讓自己被淋濕。

你不但不抱怨，還覺得很舒服。

結論：你討厭的不是被水淋，你討厭的是免費被淋。

只要付了水費，同樣的事情就變成享受。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下大雨衝回家換完衣服馬上去洗澡的時候傳給也淋成落湯雞的朋友，順便附註：剛剛我們是在抱怨水太便宜
variations: 下雨被淋叫做倒楣。洗澡被淋叫做放鬆。差別只在於這場雨是不是你付的錢。

en.content:
When it rains, you grab an umbrella, hide from it, and complain about your wet clothes.

The first thing you do when you get home is walk into the bathroom.

In the bathroom, you turn on the showerhead.

What comes out of the showerhead is, fundamentally, rain.

You stand under it and let it soak you.

You not only don't complain, you find it relaxing.

Conclusion: what you hate isn't being rained on. What you hate is being rained on for free.

The moment you pay the water bill, the exact same thing becomes a treat.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who also got drenched after you sprint home in the rain and immediately jump in the shower — note that we were basically complaining the water was too cheap
variations: Getting wet from rain is called bad luck. Getting wet from a shower is called relaxing. The only difference is who paid for the water.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：手機電量焦慮

zh-tw.content:
手機顯示剩百分之一的時候，你會立刻找充電器。

手機顯示剩百分之二十的時候，你也會找充電器。

手機顯示剩百分之五十的時候，你還是會找充電器。

手機顯示百分之百的時候，你會把它從充電器拔掉。

拔掉之後，你會看著電量慢慢往下掉。

看到一個程度，你又會把它插回去。

結論：手機的電量從來沒有真的被「用完」過，也從來沒有真的「夠了」過。

你只是花了一輩子的時間，在百分之一到百分之百之間，反覆橫跳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 出門前一邊罵手機沒電一邊到處找線的時候傳給朋友，順便附註：我們其實沒在用手機，我們只是在管理它的電
variations: 手機從買來那一天起，你就再也沒有讓它真的關過機。它只是在你的焦慮和插座之間，永遠循環。

en.content:
When your phone shows one percent, you immediately look for a charger.

When your phone shows twenty percent, you also look for a charger.

When your phone shows fifty percent, you still look for a charger.

When your phone reaches a hundred percent, you unplug it.

After unplugging it, you watch the battery slowly drop.

At some point, you plug it back in.

Conclusion: your phone's battery has never actually been fully used up, and has never actually been fully enough.

You've just spent your entire life bouncing between one percent and one hundred percent.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend while you're frantically hunting for a cable before leaving the house — note that we don't really use phones, we just manage their batteries
variations: Since the day you bought your phone, you've never actually let it die. It just loops forever between your anxiety and the nearest outlet.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：找眼鏡

zh-tw.content:
戴眼鏡的人，要找眼鏡的時候，會遇到一個問題。

那個問題就是，他沒戴眼鏡。

沒戴眼鏡的時候，他看不清楚眼鏡放在哪裡。

如果他看得清楚，他就不需要眼鏡。

如果他不需要眼鏡，他就不會在找眼鏡。

結論：找眼鏡這件事，本質上只能由不需要找眼鏡的人來完成。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在你媽喊「我的眼鏡咧」然後你發現它就掛在她頭上的時候傳給家庭群組
variations: 找眼鏡的悖論：你越需要它，越找不到它；等你找到它，你就不再需要找它了。

en.content:
When a person who wears glasses needs to find their glasses, they run into a problem.

The problem is that they aren't wearing their glasses.

Without their glasses, they can't see clearly where the glasses are.

If they could see clearly, they wouldn't need the glasses.

If they didn't need the glasses, they wouldn't be looking for them.

Conclusion: searching for glasses is fundamentally a task that can only be completed by someone who doesn't need to search for glasses.
en.editorNote: Send to the family group chat the moment your mom yells "where are my glasses" and you spot them on top of her head
variations: The glasses paradox: the more you need them, the less you can find them. The moment you find them, you no longer need to look.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：睡前滑手機

zh-tw.content:
你躺到床上，準備睡覺。

睡覺之前，你拿起手機，說只看五分鐘。

五分鐘過了之後，你還在滑。

半小時過了之後，你也還在滑。

一個小時過了之後，你開始覺得自己很累。

你覺得自己很累，是因為你沒在睡覺。

你沒在睡覺，是因為你在滑手機。

你滑手機，是因為你不想浪費時間就這樣睡著。

結論：你為了不浪費時間，浪費了一個小時。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨兩點還在床上滑短影片的時候傳給跟你同樣症頭的朋友，順便加一句：我們明天會後悔，但今天還是要繼續
variations: 「再五分鐘就睡」是人類發明過最不準確的時間單位，平均誤差大約一個小時起跳。

en.content:
You lie down in bed, ready to sleep.

Before sleeping, you pick up your phone and say it's just five minutes.

Five minutes pass, and you're still scrolling.

Half an hour passes, and you're still scrolling.

An hour passes, and you start to feel exhausted.

You feel exhausted because you aren't sleeping.

You aren't sleeping because you're scrolling.

You're scrolling because you don't want to waste the day by just falling asleep.

Conclusion: in order to not waste your time, you wasted an hour.
en.editorNote: Send at 2am to a friend who's also still scrolling shorts in bed — add: we will regret this tomorrow, but tonight, we ride
variations: "Just five more minutes" is the most inaccurate unit of time humans have ever invented. The average margin of error starts at one hour.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：冰箱

zh-tw.content:
你打開冰箱，看了一眼，沒有想吃的。

你把冰箱關上。

過了三十秒，你又打開冰箱。

冰箱裡的東西，跟剛剛一模一樣。

你還是覺得沒有想吃的。

你又把冰箱關上。

過了三十秒，你又打開。

冰箱還是那個冰箱。

結論：人類打開冰箱，並不是為了吃東西，而是為了確認裡面沒有東西可以吃。

而這個動作，每天要重複很多次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 肚子餓但又懶得出門買，第七次打開冰箱還是不知道要吃什麼的時候傳給室友，並建議他也試一次
variations: 冰箱不是儲存食物的地方，是用來反覆確認你今天還是不知道要吃什麼的儀式空間。

en.content:
You open the fridge, take a look, and find nothing you want to eat.

You close the fridge.

Thirty seconds later, you open the fridge again.

The contents of the fridge are exactly the same as before.

You still find nothing you want to eat.

You close it again.

Thirty seconds later, you open it again.

The fridge is still the same fridge.

Conclusion: humans don't open the fridge to eat. Humans open the fridge to confirm that there is nothing in it to eat.

And this confirmation must be repeated many times per day.
en.editorNote: Send to your roommate when you're hungry, too lazy to go out, and on your seventh fridge-opening of the night — recommend they try it too
variations: A fridge isn't a place to store food. It's a ritual space where you repeatedly confirm that you still have no idea what you want to eat today.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「在我忘記之前，我會一直記住的」廢話格式

zh-tw.content:
我跟你說，在我忘記之前，我都還記得。

等我想起來的時候，那就是我還沒忘。

如果我什麼時候沒講，那大概就是我忘了。

但我忘了的事情，我自己也不知道我忘了。

所以你問我有沒有忘記，我永遠只能回答：應該沒有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 另一半問你「上次我說的那件事你還記得嗎」的時候，可以拿出來救場（不一定有用）
variations: 我承諾這件事我絕對不會忘記，除非我忘記了。如果我忘記了，那也是因為我先忘記了我答應過你。

en.content:
Let me tell you: until the moment I forget, I still remember.

The moment I recall it, that means I haven't forgotten yet.

If I never bring it up, that probably means I forgot.

But the things I've forgotten, I don't know I've forgotten.

So when you ask whether I forgot, the only honest answer is: probably not.
en.editorNote: Pull this out when your partner asks if you remember the thing they told you last week — results may vary
variations: I promise I will absolutely not forget this, unless I forget. And if I forget, it's because I first forgot that I promised.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路廢話文學「講話精簡」格式

zh-tw.content:
我這個人，講話一向很簡短。

所以接下來這件事，我會長話短說。

不過這件事，說來話長。

要從很久以前開始講起。

講到後來，可能會有點離題。

但我會盡量講重點。

重點就是：這件事三言兩語說不清楚。

所以一開始我就跟你說了，我會長話短說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友問你「等等開會我簡報幾分鐘就好對吧」的時候傳給他，預告等等可能會超時三十分鐘
variations: 「我長話短說」是人類最常違反的承諾之一，僅次於「我馬上到」。

en.content:
I'm a person of few words.

So for what I'm about to tell you, I'll keep it brief.

But it's a long story.

We'll need to start from way back.

I may go off on a tangent.

But I'll do my best to stay on point.

The point is: this is not something that can be explained in a few words.

Which is why, as I said at the start, I'll keep it brief.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker who just asked "the meeting is short, right?" — fair warning it'll run thirty minutes over
variations: "I'll keep this brief" is one of the most violated promises in human history, second only to "I'm on my way."

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：搜尋找不到的東西

zh-tw.content:
東西找不到的時候，你會想問別人有沒有看到。

但你不知道東西在哪裡，是因為你沒看到它。

別人會不會看到，取決於別人有沒有經過那個地方。

如果別人也沒經過，那他也跟你一樣不知道。

如果別人有經過但沒注意，那等於沒看到。

所以最後通常會找到東西的，是你自己。

找到的時候，你會說：奇怪，剛剛明明不在這裡。

但其實它一直都在那裡。

不在的，是你的眼睛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 你又把鑰匙放在鞋櫃上忘記，在家翻箱倒櫃十分鐘終於找到的時候，把這段傳給也常常找不到東西的家人
variations: 找不到東西的人，永遠在找東西。找到東西的人，只是剛好走過去。

en.content:
When you can't find something, you want to ask other people if they've seen it.

But the reason you don't know where it is, is because you haven't seen it.

Whether someone else has seen it depends on whether they've passed by that spot.

If they haven't passed by, they're just as clueless as you.

If they passed by but didn't notice, that counts as not seeing.

So in the end, the person who finds the thing is usually you.

When you find it, you say: weird, it definitely wasn't here a second ago.

But it had always been there.

What wasn't there, was your eyes.
en.editorNote: Send to a family member who also constantly loses things, after you finally find your keys on the shoe rack where you always leave them
variations: People who can't find things spend their lives looking. People who find things just happened to walk by.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：群組已讀不回

zh-tw.content:
你在群組丟了一個訊息。

過了五分鐘，已讀十二，沒人回。

你開始想，是不是這個訊息有點冷。

但你又想，可能大家在忙。

再過五分鐘，已讀十五，還是沒人回。

你決定再丟一句「哈哈」，緩解尷尬。

結果哈哈也沒人回。

結論：群組裡的人，永遠都已讀，永遠都在忙。

他們不是不在群組，他們只是不想在群組裡說話。

而你不一樣，你發現自己也是其中之一。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在公司群組丟了一個自以為很有梗的迷因被全員已讀不回的時候，把這段傳給最好的朋友（一對一聊天，不要再丟群組）
variations: 已讀不回不是冷漠，是現代人最後的禮貌：他們選擇不浪費你看回覆的時間。

en.content:
You drop a message in the group chat.

Five minutes later: twelve people have read it, nobody replied.

You start wondering if the message was a little flat.

Then you think, maybe everyone's just busy.

Five more minutes: fifteen reads, still no replies.

You decide to send a quick "haha" to ease the awkwardness.

Nobody replies to the haha either.

Conclusion: people in group chats are eternally reading, eternally busy.

They're not absent from the group. They just don't want to speak in it.

And then you realize: you are also one of them.
en.editorNote: Send to your closest friend (in DMs, not the group) the moment your "actually pretty funny" meme gets read by everyone in the work chat and answered by no one
variations: Leaving someone on read isn't cold. It's the final courtesy of modern life: they chose not to waste your time on a reply.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路廢話文學「俗話說得好」與「具體什麼情況還得看情況」格式

zh-tw.content:
我跟你說，這件事情的情況，是一個有情況的情況。

至於是什麼情況，要看當下的情況。

當下的情況，要看你怎麼看當下的情況。

你怎麼看，會影響到情況怎麼發展。

但情況怎麼發展，又跟你怎麼看沒什麼關係。

所以結論就是：情況就是這個情況。

但具體什麼情況，還是要看情況。
zh-tw.editorNote: 主管問你「那個案子現在進度怎麼樣」但你完全沒做的時候，可以試著背出來救命（風險自負）
variations: 事情大概就是這樣的事情，至於是怎樣的事情，要看你怎麼定義「這樣」。

en.content:
Let me explain. The situation here is a situation in which a situation exists.

What kind of situation depends on the current situation.

The current situation depends on how you view the current situation.

How you view it affects how it develops.

But how it develops has nothing to do with how you view it.

So the conclusion is: the situation is what the situation is.

But as for what the situation actually is, that depends on the situation.
en.editorNote: Try reciting this when your boss asks for a project status update and you haven't started yet (use at your own risk)
variations: The thing is basically the kind of thing it is. What kind of thing? Depends entirely on how you define "the kind."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自廢話文學「越聰明的人腦子越聰明」邏輯循環格式

zh-tw.content:
我發現一件很神奇的事情：

越早睡的人，通常睡得越早。

越晚睡的人，則是睡得越晚。

睡得早的人，第二天會比較早起。

睡得晚的人，第二天會比較晚起。

但兩種人加起來的睡眠時間，可能是一樣的。

也可能不一樣。

總之，每個人的作息，都是由他自己決定的。

除非他被別人叫醒，那就是別人決定的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨兩點還在群組哈拉的朋友傳「我要去睡了」之後又繼續講話的時候，丟這段給他
variations: 「我今天要早點睡」是人類自我安慰的最高境界，僅次於「我從明天開始減肥」。

en.content:
Here's something I've noticed:

People who sleep early usually go to bed early.

People who sleep late usually go to bed late.

People who sleep early tend to wake up earlier.

People who sleep late tend to wake up later.

But the total hours of sleep for both groups might be exactly the same.

Or they might not be.

In any case, each person's schedule is decided by themselves.

Unless someone wakes them up — in which case, it's decided by someone else.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who texted "I'm going to bed" at 1am and is still replying at 2:30am
variations: "I'm going to sleep early tonight" is humanity's highest form of self-deception, second only to "I'll start the diet tomorrow."

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：自助結帳機

zh-tw.content:
你走進超市，看到自助結帳機沒人在用，覺得很方便。

你拿起第一個商品，掃條碼，機器讀到了。

你拿起第二個商品，掃條碼，機器叫你「請將商品放置於感應區」。

你已經放了。

機器再次提醒你：「請將商品放置於感應區」。

你拿起來再放一次。

機器：「未感應到商品，請呼叫店員」。

店員過來，按了一下螢幕，機器恢復正常。

店員離開後，你掃下一個商品，機器又叫店員。

結論：自助結帳機的設計目的，不是讓你自己結帳，而是讓你跟店員培養感情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 你在超商自助結帳被機器叫第三次店員、整排排在你後面的人開始嘆氣的時候，傳給也常常被自助結帳機背叛的朋友
variations: 自助結帳機唯一不能自助的，就是它本身。
排自助結帳那一排，平均要叫三次店員，但你還是覺得比一般結帳快。

en.content:
You walk into the supermarket, see an empty self-checkout machine, and feel pretty smart about it.

You scan the first item. The machine accepts it.

You scan the second item. The machine says: "Please place the item in the bagging area."

The item is already in the bagging area.

The machine repeats: "Please place the item in the bagging area."

You pick it up and put it down again.

The machine: "Item not detected. Please wait for assistance."

A staff member walks over, taps the screen once, and everything works again.

They leave. You scan the next item. The machine calls them back.

Conclusion: the purpose of self-checkout is not to let you check out by yourself. It's to help you build a relationship with the staff.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend after the self-checkout calls staff for the third time and the entire line behind you starts sighing audibly
variations: The only thing a self-checkout machine cannot do by itself, is itself.
Self-checkout calls staff three times on average — but somehow you still believe it's faster than a regular line.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：等公車

zh-tw.content:
你站在站牌前等公車。

公車到之前，你都還沒上車。

公車到了之後，你就上車了。

上車之前的時間，叫做等公車。

等公車的時間，公車不在你面前。

公車不在你面前的時候，你只能繼續等。

你看了一下手機，公車App說還有三分鐘。

三分鐘過後，公車App說還有兩分鐘。

兩分鐘過後，公車App說還有四分鐘。

你抬頭看路口，公車剛好開過去，沒有停。

結論：公車App顯示的剩餘時間，是一個用來緩解焦慮的數字，不是預測。
zh-tw.editorNote: 等公車等到第二班直接過站不停的時候，把這段傳給跟你約吃飯但你絕對會遲到的朋友
variations: 公車App上的「即將到站」，跟「我馬上到」是同義詞——都是再給我一段時間的意思。

en.content:
You stand at the bus stop, waiting for the bus.

Until the bus arrives, you have not boarded the bus.

Once the bus arrives, you board it.

The time before boarding is called waiting for the bus.

During this time, the bus is not in front of you.

While the bus is not in front of you, you can only keep waiting.

You check your phone. The bus app says three minutes.

Three minutes later, the app says two minutes.

Two minutes later, the app says four minutes.

You look up just in time to see the bus drive past your stop without stopping.

Conclusion: the time displayed by a bus app is a number designed to ease your anxiety, not a prediction.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend you're meeting for dinner (and will definitely be late for) when the second bus skips your stop entirely
variations: "Arriving soon" on a bus app means the same thing as "on my way" in a text — give me more time.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：手機電量

zh-tw.content:
手機顯示還有百分之二十電量，你覺得還可以撐一下。

半小時後，手機顯示還有百分之十九。

你覺得電池真耐用。

再過五分鐘，手機顯示百分之三。

你開始找充電線。

找到充電線的時候，手機自動關機了。

結論：手機電量百分之二十到百分之十九，是一段很長的時間；百分之十九到關機，是一個瞬間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在咖啡廳手機剩百分之三、座位附近完全沒插座的時候，傳給也常常被電量背叛的朋友
variations: 電池只有兩種狀態：還很多、沒了。中間那段顯示的數字，是給你看心安的。
百分之一可以用十分鐘，百分之二十只能用十秒——這是手機電池的規則。

en.content:
Your phone shows 20% battery. You figure you've got time.

Half an hour later, it still shows 19%.

You marvel at how good the battery is.

Five minutes later, it shows 3%.

You start looking for the charger.

By the time you find it, the phone has shut off.

Conclusion: 20% to 19% is a long stretch of time. 19% to dead is a single instant.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend the moment your phone hits 3% in a cafe with zero outlets within reach
variations: A phone battery has two states: plenty left, and gone. The numbers in between are decorative.
1% lasts ten minutes. 20% lasts ten seconds. These are the rules.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學睡眠主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
研究指出，睡覺的時候人是閉著眼睛的。

睡著之後，你就感覺不到時間在過。

等你感覺到時間在過的時候，通常已經是鬧鐘響了。

鬧鐘響了之後，你會想再睡五分鐘。

再睡五分鐘的結果，是再睡四十分鐘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在朋友傳「我今天好累要早睡」的時候，立刻回他這段，讓他知道一切都是徒勞
variations: 鬧鐘的功能是叫醒你，但通常是叫醒你之後的那個你，而不是當下的你。

en.content:
Studies have shown that during sleep, the eyes are typically closed.

Once you're asleep, you no longer perceive time passing.

By the time you perceive time passing again, it's usually because the alarm went off.

After the alarm goes off, you decide to sleep five more minutes.

The result of five more minutes is forty more minutes.
en.editorNote: When a friend texts "I'm exhausted, going to bed early tonight," reply with this so they know how it ends
variations: An alarm clock's job is to wake you up — but usually a future version of you, not the current one.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：找東西

zh-tw.content:
你要出門，找鑰匙。

你記得鑰匙放在桌上，但桌上沒有。

你找了沙發、口袋、鞋櫃、廁所、冰箱、書桌、抽屜，全部都沒有。

你坐下來深呼吸，然後再走回桌邊。

鑰匙在桌上。

結論：鑰匙的位置不會改變，會改變的是你看得到鑰匙的能力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 出門前找鑰匙找了十五分鐘、最後在原本的位置找到的時候，傳給每天上班都遲到的朋友
variations: 找東西的時候，那個東西不會出現；找到一半放棄的時候，它就會出現。
鑰匙不會自己跑掉，但你眼睛會自己跑掉。

en.content:
You're heading out. You look for your keys.

You remember leaving them on the table. They're not on the table.

You check the couch, your pockets, the shoe rack, the bathroom, the fridge, the desk, every drawer. Nothing.

You sit down, take a deep breath, and walk back to the table.

The keys are on the table.

Conclusion: the location of the keys does not change. What changes is your ability to see them.
en.editorNote: Send to your chronically late friend after spending fifteen minutes looking for your keys and finding them exactly where you started
variations: When you're searching for it, it doesn't appear. The moment you give up, it appears.
Keys don't move. Your eyes do.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學工作主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
上班的時候，你想下班。

下班的時候，你不用想，因為你已經下班了。

但是下班的時間越長，越接近上班。

等你發現的時候，已經又要上班了。

結論：下班只是兩段上班的中間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上九點打開電腦發現明天又要開會的時候，傳給同樣在過星期天症候群的同事
variations: 週末過得快，是因為它是用來等下個禮拜上班的緩衝時間。
放假的長度跟你期待它的長度成反比。

en.content:
While you're at work, you want to be off work.

While you're off work, you don't have to want it — you're already off.

But the longer you're off, the closer you get to being back at work.

By the time you notice, you're back at work.

Conclusion: being off work is just the gap between two stretches of being at work.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker on Sunday night at 9pm when you both open your laptops and see Monday's meetings looming
variations: Weekends feel short because they exist to buffer the next week of work.
The length of a vacation is inversely proportional to how much you were looking forward to it.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：減肥

zh-tw.content:
你決定要減肥。

減肥的第一步，是少吃。

少吃的意思，是吃得比平常少。

但是你今天工作很累，需要補充能量。

所以你今天吃得跟平常一樣多。

明天再開始減肥。

結論：減肥是一個從明天開始、永遠不會到今天的計畫。
zh-tw.editorNote: 宵夜叫了鹹酥雞、邊吃邊說「明天開始減肥」的時候，傳給跟你一起每週都要減肥一次的朋友
variations: 「明天開始減肥」跟「我馬上到」一樣，都是一種文學修辭。
減肥計畫的開始日，永遠在明天；結束日，通常在今天的晚餐。

en.content:
You decide to start a diet.

Step one of a diet is eating less.

Eating less means eating less than usual.

But today was a tough day at work and you need to refuel.

So today you eat the same as always.

The diet starts tomorrow.

Conclusion: a diet is a plan that begins tomorrow and never reaches today.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend you weekly co-restart your diet with — ideally while eating fried chicken at midnight saying "starting tomorrow"
variations: "Starting my diet tomorrow" is a literary device, not a plan.
A diet's start date is always tomorrow. Its end date is usually tonight's dinner.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學重複句式參考，重新創作主題：手機通知

zh-tw.content:
你的手機叮了一聲，你拿起來看。

看完之後，你把手機放下。

放下不到三秒，手機又叮了一聲。

你又拿起來看。

科學證實：放下手機跟拿起手機之間的時間，剛好等於一則通知的長度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在群組訊息一直跳通知的時候，傳出去讓大家一起放下手機（然後再拿起來）
variations: 通知的數量跟你的耐心成反比，跟你的好奇心成正比。
手機放下的那一刻，就是下一則通知準備出現的時候。

en.content:
Your phone buzzes. You pick it up to look.

You look. You put it down.

Within three seconds, it buzzes again.

You pick it up again.

Science confirms: the time between putting the phone down and picking it back up is exactly equal to the length of one notification.
en.editorNote: Drop this in a group chat that won't stop pinging — let everyone put their phones down together (and then pick them back up)
variations: The number of notifications is inversely proportional to your patience and directly proportional to your curiosity.
The moment you put your phone down is the moment the next notification prepares itself.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學嚴謹格式參考，主題：等公車

zh-tw.content:
公車還沒來的時候，你在等公車。

公車來了之後，你就不用再等公車了，因為它已經來了。

但是你下車之後，又要開始等下一班公車。

結論：等公車這件事不會結束，會結束的只有你今天的通勤。
zh-tw.editorNote: 通勤時公車誤點十五分鐘、站牌時刻表完全只是裝飾的時候，傳給每天一起等車的同事
variations: 公車的時刻表是一種建議，不是承諾。
你越趕時間，公車就越慢；你越早到，公車就越早來——但你不會早到。

en.content:
Before the bus arrives, you are waiting for the bus.

Once the bus arrives, you no longer need to wait for the bus, because it has arrived.

But after you get off, you start waiting for the next bus.

Conclusion: waiting for the bus never ends. Only your commute ends.
en.editorNote: Send to your daily commute buddy when the bus is fifteen minutes late and the schedule is purely decorative
variations: A bus schedule is a suggestion, not a commitment.
The more you're in a hurry, the later the bus is. The earlier you arrive, the earlier it comes — but you never arrive early.

items.sourceNote: 原創廢話文學，主題：洗衣服

zh-tw.content:
你把衣服丟進洗衣機。

洗衣機開始運轉，這代表衣服正在被洗。

衣服被洗完之後，就是乾淨的衣服。

但是乾淨的衣服還在洗衣機裡，所以它跟髒衣服在物理上的位置是一樣的。

你忘記拿出來。

隔天它又變成髒衣服了。

結論：洗衣服是一個迴圈，起點跟終點都是洗衣機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末打開洗衣機發現衣服又有那個味道、需要重洗一次的時候，傳給跟你一樣家事永遠做不完的朋友
variations: 晾衣服的最後一步，是把衣服收下來。但通常都是因為下雨才被迫想起來。
洗衣籃永遠不會空，因為你身上穿的那件，是下一批洗衣的預備役。

en.content:
You toss your clothes into the washer.

The washer starts. This means the clothes are being washed.

When the wash is done, the clothes are clean.

But the clean clothes are still in the washer, which means they are physically in the same place as the dirty clothes were.

You forget to take them out.

The next day, they're dirty again.

Conclusion: laundry is a loop. The starting point and ending point are both the washing machine.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose chores are also never finished — ideally on Sunday when you reopen the washer and the clothes have That Smell again
variations: The last step of hanging laundry is bringing it in. Usually you only remember because it started raining.
The laundry hamper is never empty. The shirt you're wearing is the reserve unit for the next load.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學自我引用句式，主題：說廢話

zh-tw.content:
俗話說得好：俗話說得好。

這句話聽起來很有道理，因為它本來就是俗話。

但是它沒有告訴你俗話是什麼。

你也不需要知道，因為你已經聽過了。

結論：聽君一席話，如聽一席話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友在群組裡假裝認真討論人生哲理的時候，丟這句進去重新定調氣氛
variations: 我有一句話想說，這句話就是我想說的那句話。
你說的話我都聽進去了，所以我什麼都沒記住。

en.content:
As the saying goes: as the saying goes.

This sounds wise because it is, by definition, a saying.

But it doesn't tell you what the saying is.

You don't need to know — you've already heard it.

Conclusion: hearing your words is like hearing your words.
en.editorNote: Drop this in a group chat where someone is being suspiciously philosophical, and watch the tone reset itself
variations: I have something to say, and the thing I have to say is the thing I want to say.
I heard everything you said, which is why I remember none of it.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學記憶句式延伸創作，主題：忘記

zh-tw.content:
在我忘記這件事之前，我會一直記得它。

忘了之後，我就不會記得了。

這時候你提醒我，我才會想起來原來我忘了。

結論：記得跟忘記，差別只在你有沒有被提醒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友抱怨另一半忘記紀念日的時候，傳這句過去配一張無辜的貓咪圖，效果加倍
variations: 我沒有忘記，只是還沒想起來。
你提醒我之前，我以為我記得；你提醒我之後，我確定我忘了。

en.content:
Before I forget this, I will always remember it.

After I forget, I will not remember it.

When you remind me, that's when I realize I had forgotten.

Conclusion: the only difference between remembering and forgetting is whether someone reminded you.
en.editorNote: Send this when a friend is venting about their partner forgetting an anniversary — pair with an innocent cat photo for maximum effect
variations: I didn't forget. I just haven't remembered yet.
Before you reminded me, I thought I remembered. After you reminded me, I confirmed I forgot.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學睡眠主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你睡得越晚，你睡覺的時間就越晚。

你睡覺的時間越晚，隔天起床的時間也會跟著變晚。

如果你不起床，那一天就沒有開始。

沒有開始的一天，等於沒有過完。

所以熬夜的人，其實活得比別人少一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨三點還在滑手機的時候傳給自己看，會有一點點良心發現
variations: 早睡的人比晚睡的人，提早進入睡眠狀態。這是真的。
睡覺是免費的，但你必須付出時間。

en.content:
The later you sleep, the later you go to bed.

The later you go to bed, the later you wake up.

If you don't wake up, the day hasn't started.

A day that hasn't started is a day that hasn't ended.

So people who stay up late are actually living one day less than everyone else.
en.editorNote: Send this to yourself at 3 a.m. while you're still scrolling — guilt level may slightly increase
variations: People who sleep early enter the sleep state earlier than people who sleep late. This is accurate.
Sleep is free, but you have to pay with time.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學金錢主題原創

zh-tw.content:
經濟學家指出，當你把錢花掉之後，那筆錢就不在你身上了。

那筆錢會跑到收你錢的人那裡。

如果他也把錢花掉，那筆錢又會跑到下一個人那裡。

所以錢一直在流動，只是不在你這裡。

這就是為什麼你存不到錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底錢包見底的時候傳到朋友群組，大家會一起苦笑
variations: 想存錢的第一步，是不要花錢。第二步，重複第一步。
你的薪水入帳之前，它不是你的；入帳之後，它也很快不是你的。

en.content:
Economists have pointed out that once you spend your money, the money is no longer with you.

It is now with the person you paid.

If they also spend it, it moves to the next person.

So money is always flowing — just not in your direction.

This is why you have no savings.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat at the end of the month when everyone's wallet is empty — collective sighing guaranteed
variations: Step one of saving money: don't spend it. Step two: repeat step one.
Before your paycheck arrives, it isn't yours. After it arrives, it isn't yours either, very quickly.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學工作主題原創

zh-tw.content:
上班的本質，就是去公司。

如果你沒有去公司，那就不算上班。

但是你去了公司，也不一定有在工作。

所以上班跟工作不是同一件事。

這也解釋了為什麼你坐了八小時，卻覺得什麼都沒做。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上九點半，假裝在打資料其實在打這句廢話傳給同事，是辦公室生存之道
variations: 下班的前提是有上班，沒上班就沒有所謂下班。
開會的目的是討論事情，但通常開完會還是要再開一次會。

en.content:
The essence of going to work is showing up at the office.

If you don't show up, it doesn't count as work.

But showing up doesn't mean you actually worked.

So going to work and doing work are not the same thing.

This is why you sat there for eight hours and feel like you did nothing.
en.editorNote: Monday at 9:30 a.m., pretend to type a report while actually typing this to a coworker — essential office survival skill
variations: Clocking out requires clocking in. No clock-in, no clock-out.
The point of a meeting is to discuss things, but it usually leads to another meeting.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學社交主題原創

zh-tw.content:
你跟一個人很熟，代表你們很熟。

如果你們不熟，那就是不熟。

所以判斷你們熟不熟，最簡單的方法就是看你們熟不熟。

這個方法雖然繞，但目前沒有更準確的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被問到「你跟那個誰很熟嗎」的時候，回這句保證對方不知道該怎麼接
variations: 朋友就是會跟你變朋友的人，沒變成朋友的就還不是朋友。
你認識的人裡面，有一些是熟人，剩下的就是不熟的人。

en.content:
If you're close with someone, it means you're close.

If you're not close, then you're not close.

So the easiest way to tell whether you're close is to check whether you're close.

The method is circular, but currently there's no more accurate one.
en.editorNote: When someone asks 'are you close with that person?' — drop this and watch them malfunction
variations: A friend is someone who has become your friend. Anyone who hasn't, isn't yet.
Among the people you know, some are close to you. The rest are not.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學戀愛主題原創

zh-tw.content:
戀愛專家指出，談戀愛需要兩個人。

如果只有一個人，那叫單戀。

如果三個人，那叫麻煩。

所以兩個人剛剛好。

但這並不代表兩個人就會在一起，也不代表在一起就會幸福。

總之，戀愛是一件很複雜的事，因為它不簡單。
zh-tw.editorNote: 情人節單身傳給單身朋友，雙方都會獲得一種沒有用的安慰
variations: 喜歡一個人很容易，被一個人喜歡也很容易，但兩件事同時發生就不容易了。
你愛的人不一定愛你，愛你的人你也不一定愛，所以單身才是常態。

en.content:
Relationship experts have confirmed that dating requires two people.

With only one person, it's a crush.

With three, it's a problem.

So two is the right number.

But this doesn't mean two people will end up together, nor that being together means happiness.

In short, love is complicated, because it isn't simple.
en.editorNote: Forward to a single friend on Valentine's Day — you'll both feel a uniquely useless comfort
variations: Liking someone is easy. Being liked is easy. Both happening at once — not easy.
The person you love may not love you. The person who loves you, you may not love. This is why being single is the default.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學運動健身主題原創

zh-tw.content:
想要變瘦的人，必須做兩件事：少吃跟多動。

少吃就是不要吃太多，多動就是不要動太少。

如果你沒做這兩件事，那你就不會變瘦。

如果你做了，也不一定會變瘦。

但如果你連做都沒做，那肯定不會變瘦。

所以要不要做，看你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 辦了健身房年費卡卻沒去過第二次的朋友，傳這句最有共鳴
variations: 運動完會流汗的人，比沒運動的人多流了一點汗。
減肥最有效的方法，就是真的去減肥。

en.content:
To lose weight, you must do two things: eat less and move more.

Eat less means don't eat too much. Move more means don't move too little.

If you don't do these two things, you won't lose weight.

If you do, you might still not lose weight.

But if you don't even try, you definitely won't.

So whether to do it is up to you.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who bought a gym membership and only went once — peak relatability
variations: People who exercise tend to sweat more than people who don't.
The most effective way to lose weight is to actually lose weight.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學手機網路主題原創

zh-tw.content:
你的手機沒電的時候，會自動關機。

關機之後，你就沒辦法滑手機了。

沒辦法滑手機的時候，你就會去找充電器。

找到充電器之後，你又可以繼續滑手機。

所以手機的續航能力，跟充電器的存在息息相關。
zh-tw.editorNote: 手機剩5%電量的時候傳到群組，傳完手機就關機了，效果絕佳
variations: Wi-Fi 連得上的時候叫網路，連不上的時候叫人生。
已讀不回的人，通常是已讀了卻沒回。

en.content:
When your phone runs out of battery, it shuts down.

Once it shuts down, you can't scroll on it.

When you can't scroll, you look for a charger.

Once you find one, you can scroll again.

So your phone's battery life is closely tied to whether the charger exists.
en.editorNote: Send this to the group chat when you're at 5 percent — your phone dying right after is the perfect punctuation
variations: When the Wi-Fi works, it's the internet. When it doesn't, it's life.
People who read your message but don't reply have, in fact, read it without replying.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學購物主題原創

zh-tw.content:
購物的本質，就是把錢換成東西。

換完之後，錢變少，東西變多。

如果你後悔了，可以把東西換回成錢，這叫退貨。

但通常退貨會比購物麻煩，所以大部分人選擇留著東西。

這就是為什麼你家越來越擠，錢包卻越來越扁。
zh-tw.editorNote: 雙11剁完手回過神來的時候傳給戰友們，療傷效果一流
variations: 促銷就是把貴的東西，標上一個比較不貴的價錢。
你買的東西用得到的話叫必需品，用不到的話叫收藏。

en.content:
The essence of shopping is trading money for things.

After the trade, you have less money and more things.

If you regret it, you can trade the things back for money — that's called a return.

But returns are usually more annoying than buying, so most people keep the things.

This is why your home keeps getting more crowded while your wallet keeps getting thinner.
en.editorNote: Send to fellow casualties after a Black Friday haul — instant therapy
variations: A sale is putting a slightly less expensive price tag on an expensive item.
If you use what you bought, it's a necessity. If you don't, it's a collection.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學上班通勤主題原創

zh-tw.content:
上班之所以叫上班，是因為你必須去上班。

不去上班的話，就不叫上班，叫請假。

請假請太多次的話，就不叫請假，叫離職。

離職之後就不用上班了，但你也沒有錢了。

沒有錢的話，就得再去找一個地方上班。

所以人生就是一個上班、不上班、再上班的循環。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上塞在捷運裡的時候打開來看，會有一種命運共同體的釋懷感
variations: 通勤的意義，就是把你從家裡搬到公司，再從公司搬回家。
週一之所以難過，是因為它後面接著週二。

en.content:
Work is called work because you have to go to work.

If you don't go, it's not work — it's called taking a day off.

If you take too many days off, it's not a day off anymore — it's called quitting.

After quitting, you don't have to work, but you also don't have money.

Without money, you'll need to find somewhere new to work.

So life is a loop of working, not working, and working again.
en.editorNote: Open this while stuck on a packed Monday morning train — instant solidarity with the rest of humanity
variations: The point of commuting is moving you from home to the office, and then back from the office to home.
Mondays are hard because Tuesday is right behind them.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學吃飯主題原創

zh-tw.content:
肚子餓的時候去吃飯，吃完就不餓了。

但是過幾個小時之後，肚子又會餓。

所以吃飯這件事，是無法一次解決的。

科學家研究發現，這個現象從人類出現以來，就一直存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 中午問同事吃什麼，對方說隨便的時候，可以丟這段給他思考
variations: 好吃的東西吃起來都好吃，不好吃的剛好相反。
吃到飽的店之所以叫吃到飽，是因為你不吃到飽就虧了。

en.content:
When you're hungry, you eat, and then you're not hungry.

But a few hours later, you're hungry again.

So eating is something you can't solve in one go.

Scientists have confirmed this phenomenon has existed since humans first appeared.
en.editorNote: When a coworker says 'anything' for lunch, send this and let them sit with it
variations: Delicious things taste delicious. Non-delicious things do the opposite.
All-you-can-eat is called all-you-can-eat because if you don't eat all you can, you lose.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學睡眠主題原創

zh-tw.content:
晚上不睡的人，早上會起不來。

早上起不來的人，上班會遲到。

上班遲到的人，會被老闆罵。

被老闆罵的人，晚上會睡不著。

睡不著的人，晚上就不睡。

這就是失眠的科學。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨三點滑手機滑到一半，傳到群組讓其他失眠的朋友一起共鳴
variations: 睡眠不足的人，通常是睡眠時數不夠。
床很好睡，但只有在你該起床的時候。

en.content:
People who don't sleep at night can't get up in the morning.

People who can't get up in the morning are late for work.

People who are late for work get yelled at by their boss.

People who get yelled at by their boss can't sleep at night.

People who can't sleep at night don't sleep at night.

This is the science of insomnia.
en.editorNote: Send this at 3 a.m. to the group chat so your fellow insomniacs feel seen
variations: People who don't get enough sleep usually haven't slept enough.
The bed is great for sleeping — but only when you're supposed to be getting up.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學朋友友情主題原創

zh-tw.content:
有朋友的人，比較不孤單。

沒有朋友的人，比較孤單。

所以為了不孤單，你需要朋友。

但朋友也是人，他們也有自己的事要忙。

所以就算有朋友，有時候你還是會孤單。

這就是為什麼人類發明了手機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末晚上一個人在家，傳給已讀不回的朋友剛剛好
variations: 好朋友就是會聯絡的朋友，不聯絡的就變成前朋友。
朋友圈越大，能講真心話的人越少。

en.content:
People with friends are less lonely.

People without friends are more lonely.

So to not be lonely, you need friends.

But friends are people too, and they have their own things going on.

So even with friends, you'll sometimes still be lonely.

This is why humans invented smartphones.
en.editorNote: Saturday night alone at home? Send this to the friend who left you on read — perfect passive-aggression
variations: A good friend is a friend you keep in touch with. The ones you don't become former friends.
The bigger your friend circle, the fewer people you can really talk to.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學上班開會主題原創

zh-tw.content:
開會是為了討論事情。

如果事情討論完了，就不用開會。

但是事情通常不會在會議裡討論完。

所以就要再開一次會。

這就是為什麼會議永遠開不完。
zh-tw.editorNote: 禮拜一早上九點，第三場線上會議開始前，貼到部門群組讓大家一起苦笑
variations: 這個會議本來可以是一封 Email，但是寄信也是要花時間的。
會議結論就是下次再開會討論。

en.content:
Meetings exist to discuss things.

If the things were already discussed, you wouldn't need the meeting.

But things rarely get fully discussed in a meeting.

So you need another meeting.

This is why meetings never end.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the team Slack right before the third Monday morning standup of the day
variations: This meeting could have been an email — but writing emails also takes time.
The conclusion of every meeting is to schedule another meeting.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學金錢主題原創

zh-tw.content:
有錢的人比較不缺錢。

沒錢的人比較缺錢。

所以想要不缺錢，就要有錢。

但是要有錢，就必須先賺錢。

而賺錢需要花時間，花時間就無法享受人生。

所以有錢人和沒錢人，都沒辦法好好享受人生。

這就是經濟學。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底發薪水前一天，跟同事一起怨嘆人生時的萬用結語
variations: 存錢的方法很簡單，就是不要把錢花掉。
錢不是萬能的，但是沒有錢是萬萬不能的，所以錢還是滿能的。

en.content:
Rich people have less of a money problem.

Poor people have more of a money problem.

So to not have a money problem, you need money.

But to have money, you first have to earn money.

And earning money takes time, and time is what you need to enjoy life.

So rich people and poor people both can't really enjoy life.

This is economics.
en.editorNote: The day before payday, send this to your group chat so everyone can complain together
variations: The trick to saving money is simply to not spend it.
Money isn't everything, but having no money is nothing, so money is actually pretty something.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學運動健身主題原創

zh-tw.content:
運動的人比較會流汗。

不運動的人比較不會流汗。

所以如果你不想流汗，可以不要運動。

但是不運動會變胖。

變胖之後想要瘦下來，就必須運動。

所以最好的辦法，就是一開始就不要胖。

感謝你閱讀這篇健身指南。
zh-tw.editorNote: 辦了健身房會員卡但只去過兩次的你，轉發給同樣偷懶的朋友
variations: 健身房辦了不去，等於沒辦。但是辦了沒去也比沒辦好，因為至少有想過。
跑步可以讓你變健康，前提是你真的有去跑。

en.content:
People who exercise sweat more.

People who don't exercise sweat less.

So if you don't want to sweat, just don't exercise.

But not exercising will make you gain weight.

And if you want to lose weight, you have to exercise.

So the best strategy is to never gain weight in the first place.

Thank you for reading this fitness guide.
en.editorNote: For when you bought a gym membership and went twice — forward to your equally lazy friend
variations: A gym membership you don't use is the same as no membership. But it's still better than not having one, because at least you thought about it.
Running makes you healthy — provided you actually go running.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學手機社群主題原創

zh-tw.content:
手機沒電的時候，要充電。

充電的時候，沒辦法滑手機。

沒辦法滑手機的時候，會覺得無聊。

覺得無聊的時候，會更想滑手機。

結果就是，你會一直盯著充電中的手機。

科學家把這個現象稱為現代生活。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看著手機從 1% 慢慢充到 3% 的時候，發到限時動態剛剛好
variations: 已讀不回的人，通常是已讀但是沒有回。
通知關掉了，但你還是會一直打開來看有沒有通知。

en.content:
When your phone is out of battery, you have to charge it.

While it's charging, you can't scroll on it.

When you can't scroll, you get bored.

When you're bored, you want to scroll even more.

So you end up staring at your charging phone.

Scientists call this phenomenon modern life.
en.editorNote: Perfect for your story while you watch your phone slowly climb from 1% to 3%
variations: People who leave you on read have usually read it but not replied.
You turned off notifications, but you still keep opening the app to check for notifications.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學密碼帳號主題原創

zh-tw.content:
為了帳號安全，密碼要設複雜一點。

密碼設複雜了，自己會記不住。

記不住就只好寫下來。

寫下來之後，就跟沒設一樣。

所以最安全的密碼，就是你也忘記的那一組。
zh-tw.editorNote: 第三次點「忘記密碼」的時候，傳給也在跟密碼搏鬥的同事
variations: 你的密碼必須包含大寫、小寫、數字、符號，以及你絕對不會記得的東西。
驗證碼簡訊還沒到的時候，驗證碼就過期了。

en.content:
For account security, your password needs to be complex.

If your password is complex, you won't remember it.

If you can't remember it, you'll write it down.

Once you write it down, you might as well not have set one.

So the safest password is the one you also forgot.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker after you click 'Forgot password?' for the third time today
variations: Your password must contain uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols, and something you will absolutely not remember.
By the time the verification code text arrives, the code has already expired.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學假日耍廢主題原創

zh-tw.content:
週末就是要好好休息。

好好休息的方法，就是什麼都不做。

什麼都不做之後，會覺得這個週末什麼都沒做。

覺得什麼都沒做，就會更累。

所以為了不要更累，下週末要好好做點事。

結果下週末又什麼都沒做。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上躺在床上滑手機，準備迎接禮拜一焦慮的時候轉發
variations: 假日睡到中午，等於少放了半天假。
說好要去走走的週末，最後還是走到便利商店就回家。

en.content:
The weekend is for resting.

The best way to rest is to do nothing.

After doing nothing, you'll feel like the weekend was wasted.

Feeling like you wasted it makes you more tired.

So next weekend you should actually do something.

Next weekend, you also do nothing.
en.editorNote: For Sunday night doom-scrolling in bed, right before the Monday dread kicks in
variations: Sleeping in until noon on a day off is basically losing half a day off.
The weekend you swore you'd go out, you made it to the convenience store and came home.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學等公車主題原創

zh-tw.content:
公車有來，就會到站。

公車沒來，就不會到站。

所以你在站牌等的，是會來的那一班。

至於不會來的那一班，你會等到天黑也等不到。

而通常，你等的就是不會來的那一班。
zh-tw.editorNote: App 顯示「將進站」結果消失的時候，發到群組討拍
variations: 公車要等的時候不來，不等的時候一次來三班。
你看著公車從你眼前開走，這就是公車。

en.content:
If the bus is coming, it will arrive.

If the bus isn't coming, it won't arrive.

So the one you're waiting for is the one that will come.

The one that won't come, you can wait for until nightfall.

And usually, the one you're waiting for is the one that won't come.
en.editorNote: Send to your group chat after the app says 'Approaching' and then the bus disappears
variations: Buses never come when you wait, then three show up the moment you stop waiting.
You watch the bus drive past you. That's a bus.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學衣櫃穿搭主題原創

zh-tw.content:
衣櫃裡的衣服很多。

但是可以穿的沒幾件。

所以每天都在煩惱要穿什麼。

煩惱完之後，還是穿那幾件。

證明衣櫃裡其實沒有衣服，只有沒穿的那幾件。
zh-tw.editorNote: 出門前在鏡子前面換第五套衣服的時候，傳給同樣有衣服焦慮的朋友
variations: 新買的衣服，穿過一次就變成舊衣服。
想穿的那件還沒洗，洗好的那件不想穿。

en.content:
There are lots of clothes in the closet.

But almost none of them are wearable.

So every morning you struggle with what to wear.

After struggling, you wear the same few pieces.

Proving the closet has no clothes — only the ones you don't wear.
en.editorNote: For when you've changed outfits five times in front of the mirror — send to a fellow closet-overflow sufferer
variations: A new shirt becomes an old shirt the first time you wear it.
The one you want to wear isn't washed; the one that's washed, you don't want to wear.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學充電線主題原創

zh-tw.content:
充電線插上去，手機才會充電。

沒插上去，就不會充電。

但是有時候插上去，也不會充電。

這時候要把線拔掉，再插一次。

如果還是不行，就再拔再插。

科技的進步，讓我們從插一次進化到插很多次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨三點發現手機沒充到電的時候，邊罵邊發給工程師朋友
variations: 充電線會在你最不需要的時候充得很快，最需要的時候根本沒接好。
買新的充電線，是為了找到舊充電線的時候才會發生的事。

en.content:
Plug in the charger and your phone will charge.

Don't plug it in and it won't charge.

But sometimes you plug it in and it still doesn't charge.

Then you unplug it and plug it back in.

If that doesn't work, unplug and plug again.

Technology has evolved us from plugging in once to plugging in many times.
en.editorNote: Discovered at 3am that your phone didn't actually charge — vent this to your engineer friend
variations: Chargers charge fastest when you don't need them and aren't connected when you do.
Buying a new charging cable is what makes the old one finally show up.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「我知道你很急」格式重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我知道你很急。

但你先別急。

因為你急也沒有用。

沒用的事情，急了就會更沒用。

所以你越急，事情就越往不急的方向發展。

結論：不急的人，比較不會急。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友訊息一直催你回覆的時候，原封不動傳回去，保證對方氣到笑
variations: 急的事情，因為很急，所以更不能急。
你急沒用，我急也沒用，所以我們可以一起不急。

en.content:
I know you're in a rush.

But don't rush.

Because rushing won't help.

When things don't help, rushing makes them help even less.

So the more you rush, the more things move in the unrushed direction.

Conclusion: people who don't rush are less likely to rush.
en.editorNote: Send back to a friend spamming you to reply faster — they'll laugh through their rage
variations: Urgent things are urgent, which is exactly why you can't rush them.
You rushing doesn't help. Me rushing doesn't help. So let's not rush together.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「沒錢」主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你知道我為什麼這麼窮嗎？

因為我沒有錢。

那為什麼沒有錢呢？

因為錢都花掉了。

那為什麼會花掉呢？

因為一開始就沒幾塊。

所以結論是，窮的根本原因，就是不富。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底跟朋友吃飯被問為什麼不點貴的時候，認真回答這段，氣氛瞬間到位
variations: 想要變有錢，第一步就是先有錢。
存款不夠是因為花太多，花太多是因為錢太少。

en.content:
You know why I'm so broke?

Because I have no money.

Why do I have no money?

Because I spent it.

Why did I spend it?

Because there wasn't much to begin with.

Conclusion: the root cause of being poor is not being rich.
en.editorNote: End-of-month dinner, friend asks why you didn't order the expensive thing — deliver this with a straight face
variations: Step one of getting rich is having money.
My savings are low because I spent too much. I spent too much because I had so little.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「越過山」格式重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
當你跨越了一座山，
你就擁有了跨越那座山的能力。

當你失敗了一次，
你就累積了一次失敗的經驗。

當你讀完了這句話，
你就讀完了這句話。

人生的成長，從來不會錯過任何一個它已經發生過的瞬間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 假裝在發人生感悟，貼到限時動態，看朋友按愛心還是已讀不回
variations: 每一次的努力，都是一次努力。
當你開始，你就已經開始了。當你結束，你就已經結束了。

en.content:
When you cross a mountain,
you possess the ability to cross that mountain.

When you fail once,
you've gained one experience of failing.

When you finish reading this sentence,
you've finished reading this sentence.

Life's growth never misses a single moment that has already happened.
en.editorNote: Post this as a fake-deep story and watch which friends double-tap vs. silently judge
variations: Every effort you make is an effort that has been made.
When you start, you've started. When you finish, you've finished.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「呼吸減壽」格式原創

zh-tw.content:
人每睡覺八小時，就會少八小時的清醒時間。

人每清醒十六小時，就會少十六小時的睡覺時間。

所以人活著，不是在睡覺，就是在不睡覺。

這個發現，目前還沒有被推翻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠到三點滑手機的時候，傳到群組裡幻想大家也沒睡
variations: 想睡的時候睡不著，不想睡的時候很想睡。
早點睡的人，會比晚點睡的人，早一點睡著。

en.content:
For every eight hours you sleep, you lose eight hours of being awake.

For every sixteen hours you're awake, you lose sixteen hours of sleeping.

So to be alive is to be either sleeping or not sleeping.

This finding has yet to be refuted.
en.editorNote: 3am scrolling, can't sleep — drop in the group chat hoping someone else is also up
variations: Can't sleep when you want to, can't stay awake when you have to.
People who sleep early fall asleep earlier than people who sleep late.

items.sourceNote: 打工人廢話文學職場格式重新創作

zh-tw.content:
老闆說：「這個專案很急。」

我點頭，因為不急的話就不會說急了。

老闆又說：「但也不能因為急就出錯。」

我繼續點頭，因為出錯了就不是沒出錯。

最後老闆說：「總之，你懂我的意思。」

我懂，我懂他以為我懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上開會被老闆訓話後，傳給同事一起翻白眼
variations: 上班的意義，就是有班可上。沒班可上的時候，就叫沒上班。
老闆說「再加把勁」的時候，意思就是要再加一把勁。

en.content:
Boss says: "This project is urgent."

I nod, because if it weren't urgent, he wouldn't say it was urgent.

Boss adds: "But being urgent is no excuse for mistakes."

I keep nodding, because making mistakes is, in fact, not making no mistakes.

Finally he says: "Anyway, you get what I mean."

I get it. I get that he thinks I get it.
en.editorNote: Monday morning standup, boss just finished a pep talk — forward this to your coworker for a shared eye-roll
variations: The point of having a job is to have a job. When you don't have one, that's called not having one.
When the boss says 'push harder,' he means push harder.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學自我重複格式原創

zh-tw.content:
單身的人，通常沒有對象。

有對象的人，通常不單身。

如果一個有對象的人覺得自己很單身，
那他可能不是單身，而是有對象但沒人陪。

結論：一個人寂寞，兩個人也可以寂寞。
zh-tw.editorNote: 情人節限定文案，傳給單身朋友安慰，傳給有伴的朋友戳痛
variations: 脫單的方式很簡單，就是不要單身。
想談戀愛的前提，是要有一個願意跟你談的人。

en.content:
Single people typically don't have a partner.

People with partners typically aren't single.

If someone with a partner still feels single,
they're probably not single — just partnered and lonely.

Conclusion: one person can be lonely. So can two.
en.editorNote: Valentine's Day special — send to single friends as comfort, send to coupled friends as a gentle stab
variations: The easiest way to stop being single is to not be single.
The prerequisite for dating someone is having someone willing to date you.

items.sourceNote: useless wisdom tautology 格式重新創作

zh-tw.content:
貴的東西，通常都比較貴。

便宜的東西，通常都比較便宜。

買得起的人，通常買得起；買不起的人，通常買不起。

所以購物的本質，是把錢換成東西，再把東西放在家裡，然後想念那些錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 結帳前猶豫要不要買的時候，先念一次冷靜冷靜
variations: 東西貴不是它的錯，是我們窮。
省錢的最好方法，就是不要花錢。

en.content:
Expensive things tend to be more expensive.

Cheap things tend to be cheaper.

People who can afford it usually can. People who can't, usually can't.

So shopping is really just converting money into objects, placing those objects in your home, and then missing the money.
en.editorNote: Read this to yourself at checkout before deciding whether to actually buy the thing
variations: It's not the item's fault for being expensive. It's our fault for being broke.
The best way to save money is to not spend it.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學嚴謹格式重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
醫生說，多運動有益健康。

因為運動的人，比不運動的人，多運動。

多運動的人，會流汗。

會流汗的人，比不會流汗的人，更會流汗。

所以結論是：想要健康，就要做一個會流汗的、會運動的、健康的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 假裝在分享養生知識，貼到家族群組看長輩認真回應
variations: 想要早起，第一步就是不要晚起。
減肥的關鍵在於少吃，少吃的關鍵在於不吃那麼多。

en.content:
Doctors say exercise is good for your health.

Because people who exercise, compared to people who don't, exercise more.

People who exercise more tend to sweat.

People who sweat tend to sweat more than people who don't.

Conclusion: to be healthy, simply become a sweating, exercising, healthy person.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the family group chat pretending to share wellness wisdom — watch the aunts reply seriously
variations: Step one to waking up early is not waking up late.
The key to losing weight is eating less. The key to eating less is not eating as much.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學睡眠主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
睡眠專家指出，一個人只要躺在床上、閉上眼睛、進入睡眠，就會睡著。

如果沒有睡著，通常是因為還沒睡著。

所以失眠的根本原因，是你還沒有睡著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜兩點傳給失眠的朋友，恭喜你又害他多醒了三十分鐘
variations: 想睡好覺的第一步，就是不要醒著。
睡得好的人，比睡不好的人，睡得好。這是常識。

en.content:
Sleep experts confirm that as long as a person lies down, closes their eyes, and falls asleep, they will be asleep.

If they are not asleep, it's usually because they haven't fallen asleep yet.

Therefore, the root cause of insomnia is that you haven't fallen asleep.
en.editorNote: Send this at 2 AM to your insomniac friend — congratulations, you just stole another thirty minutes of their night
variations: Step one to sleeping well: don't stay awake.
People who sleep well sleep better than people who don't sleep well. This is common knowledge.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學工作主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我發現，只要上班，就會上到班。

而下班之後，通常就不是上班時間了。

至於假日，多半發生在不用上班的日子裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上九點傳到公司群組，主管已讀，你的同事們會默默感謝你
variations: 加班的人，比沒加班的人，下班晚一點。
薪水是發給有上班的人。沒上班的，通常領不到。

en.content:
I've noticed that as long as you go to work, you end up at work.

After work, however, is usually not work hours.

As for weekends, they tend to occur on days you don't have to work.
en.editorNote: Send this in the office Slack at 9 AM Monday — your manager will read it, your coworkers will silently thank you
variations: People who stay late finish work later than people who don't stay late.
Paychecks go to people who show up. People who don't show up usually don't get one.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學金錢主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
經濟學家研究指出：一個人只要把錢花掉，錢就會變少。

沒花的錢，則會留下來。

所以想存錢的關鍵，就是不要把錢花光。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給說要存錢卻每天點外送的朋友，順便附上點餐連結
variations: 有錢的人，比沒錢的人，多了一些錢。
想變有錢，第一步就是先不要把錢花完。

en.content:
Economists have determined that as long as a person spends money, they have less of it.

Money that isn't spent remains.

Therefore, the key to saving money is to not spend it all.
en.editorNote: Send to your friend who keeps saying they'll save money but orders DoorDash daily — bonus points if you attach a takeout link
variations: Rich people have more money than poor people. This has been verified.
Step one to getting rich is not running out of money.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學社交主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
心理學家發現，當一個人不講話的時候，他通常是沉默的。

一旦開口，沉默就會結束。

所以想要安靜，最有效的方法，就是大家都不要說話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在尷尬的家族聚餐中默念，效果跟說出來一樣什麼都沒解決
variations: 話多的人，比話少的人，講了更多的話。
想要朋友，第一步就是認識人。沒認識的人，通常不是你的朋友。

en.content:
Psychologists have observed that when a person isn't speaking, they are usually silent.

Once they open their mouth, the silence ends.

Therefore, the most effective way to achieve quiet is for everyone to not speak.
en.editorNote: Recite silently during awkward family dinners — equally ineffective as saying it out loud, but at least you tried
variations: People who talk a lot say more words than people who talk less.
Step one to having friends is meeting people. People you haven't met are usually not your friends.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學寵物主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
獸醫指出：養貓的人，家裡通常會有一隻貓。

如果家裡沒有貓，那大概率是還沒養。

至於那些不想養貓的人，他們的家裡，通常也沒有貓。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給每天在群組瘋狂分享貓咪照片的朋友，提醒他你已經知道他有貓了
variations: 貓在家睡覺的時候，通常不會跑出去。出去的那隻，多半已經不在家。
想當貓奴的第一步，就是先有一隻貓。沒有貓的，叫做嚮往者。

en.content:
Veterinarians note: people who own a cat usually have a cat at home.

If there's no cat at home, it's most likely because they haven't gotten one yet.

As for people who don't want a cat, their home usually doesn't have one either.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who floods the group chat with cat photos daily — just to confirm, yes, you know they have a cat
variations: When a cat is sleeping at home, it usually isn't outside. The one outside is usually not at home.
Step one to becoming a cat owner is having a cat. Those without one are simply aspiring.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學旅行主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
旅遊專家研究指出：只要出國，你就會出國。

回國之後，你就回到國內了。

至於沒出國的人，他們大多都還待在原本的國家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給連假前在限動瘋狂預告要出國的朋友，順便恭喜他即將完成這項偉大壯舉
variations: 搭飛機的人，通常會比走路的人更快到目的地。除非飛機誤點。
想看世界的第一步，就是離開家。不離開的，看到的只有家。

en.content:
Travel experts have determined: as long as you go abroad, you are abroad.

Once you return, you're back in your country.

As for those who don't travel abroad, most of them are still in their original country.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been hyping their upcoming trip for three weeks straight — congratulate them on this magnificent achievement
variations: People on planes usually reach their destination faster than people walking. Unless the flight is delayed.
Step one to seeing the world is leaving home. People who don't leave only see home.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學閱讀主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
教育學家發現：一本書，只要被讀完，就是讀完了。

沒讀完的書，通常還有沒讀的部分。

所以提升閱讀量最有效的方法，就是把書打開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給今年立志要讀完二十本書、結果現在還停在第一本第三頁的朋友
variations: 看完的書，比沒看完的書，多看了幾頁。
想當文青的第一步，就是先買書。買了不看的，叫做收藏家。

en.content:
Educators have observed: as long as a book is finished, it is finished.

Unfinished books usually have parts that haven't been read.

Therefore, the most effective way to read more is to open the book.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who set a New Year's goal of reading 20 books and is still on page 3 of the first one
variations: Books that are finished have more pages read than books that aren't.
Step one to looking literary is buying books. People who buy and don't read are called collectors.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學夏天主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
氣象學家指出：夏天的時候，天氣通常比較熱。

如果不熱，那大概不是夏天。

至於冬天，研究顯示它跟夏天不太一樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在五月底全台破三十五度的時候傳到家族群組，奶奶會回你「真的耶」
variations: 開冷氣的房間，比沒開冷氣的房間，涼一點。這是物理。
想消暑的第一步，就是不要待在太陽底下。曬到的，多半會熱。

en.content:
Meteorologists note: in summer, the weather is usually hotter.

If it's not hot, it's probably not summer.

As for winter, research shows it's somewhat different from summer.
en.editorNote: Perfect to post when it hits 95F in May and everyone in the group chat is melting — your aunt will reply 'so true'
variations: Rooms with the AC on are cooler than rooms without it. This is physics.
Step one to staying cool is not standing in the sun. People in the sun tend to feel hot.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學追劇主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
影評人觀察指出：一部劇看完最後一集之後，這部劇就看完了。

還沒看完的，通常還有沒看的集數。

至於說「下一集就睡」的人，他們的睡眠時間多半比預期晚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給昨天說「就再看一集」結果天亮才睡的朋友，附上一個微笑
variations: 追劇的人，比沒追劇的人，看了更多集。
想早睡的第一步，就是不要打開Netflix。打開的，通常會看到三點。

en.content:
TV critics observe: once you watch the final episode of a series, the series is over.

Unfinished series usually have episodes you haven't watched.

As for people who say 'just one more episode,' they typically go to sleep later than planned.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who said 'just one more' last night and watched until sunrise — attach a smiling emoji
variations: People who binge-watch have seen more episodes than people who don't.
Step one to going to bed early is not opening Netflix. People who open it usually stay up until 3 AM.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學職場通勤主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
人資專家分析：每天到公司打卡的人，通常都有在上班。

沒有到公司的，多半是放假，或是還沒到。

至於遲到的同事，他們抵達公司的時間，比規定時間晚了一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 禮拜一早上傳到公司群組，主管已讀不回的機率高達九成
variations: 上班的第一步，就是要先出門。沒出門的人，通常還在床上。
捷運比走路快，這是已經被通勤族驗證過的事實。

en.content:
HR experts confirm: people who clock in at the office every day are usually at work.

Those who aren't at the office are either on leave or haven't arrived yet.

As for colleagues who are late, they tend to arrive after the scheduled time.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the company Slack on Monday morning — 90% chance your manager will leave it on read
variations: Step one of going to work is leaving the house. People who haven't left are usually still in bed.
The subway is faster than walking. This has been verified by commuters.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學金錢主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
理財專家指出：戶頭裡有錢的人，比戶頭裡沒錢的人，多了一些錢。

想要變有錢的第一步，就是先有錢。

沒錢的話，會稍微難一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底收到帳單那天傳給朋友，附上一個哭笑不得的表情
variations: 花錢的速度，通常比賺錢快。這就是為什麼戶頭會變空。
存款增加最快的方法，就是不要花錢。不花的話，數字會比較好看。

en.content:
Financial experts point out: people with money in their account have more than people without.

Step one to becoming rich is to have money.

Without it, things get slightly harder.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend on the day your credit card bill arrives — attach a crying-laughing face
variations: Spending money tends to happen faster than earning it. That's why accounts go empty.
The fastest way to grow your savings is to not spend. If you don't spend, the number looks nicer.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學睡眠主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
睡眠專家研究發現：睡著的人，通常處於睡眠狀態。

還沒睡著的，那就是還醒著。

至於說「我只是閉眼休息」的人，他們的睡眠時數，通常比自己以為的多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨兩點傳給也睡不著的朋友，沒回的可能是真的睡了
variations: 想睡飽的第一步，就是要睡得夠。睡不夠的，通常會覺得累。
賴床的人，比準時起床的人，在床上待得久一點。

en.content:
Sleep researchers have found: people who are asleep are usually in a sleeping state.

Those who aren't asleep are simply awake.

As for people who say 'I'm just resting my eyes,' their sleep duration is usually longer than they think.
en.editorNote: Text this to a friend at 2 AM — if they don't reply, they probably actually fell asleep
variations: Step one to feeling well-rested is sleeping enough. People who don't usually feel tired.
People who hit snooze stay in bed longer than people who get up on time.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學運動健身主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
健身教練表示：有去健身房的人，比沒去的人，多運動了一些。

沒去的，通常維持原本的狀態。

至於辦了會員卻沒去的人，他們的會員卡，目前還是會員卡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給辦了健身房年費結果只去過兩次的朋友，記得保持距離
variations: 想瘦的第一步，就是不要變胖。已經胖的，要往反方向努力。
跑步的人，比沒跑步的人，多跑了一些路。這就是運動。

en.content:
Personal trainers confirm: people who go to the gym work out more than people who don't.

Those who don't go usually stay in their original state.

As for people who bought a membership but never went, their membership card is still a membership card.
en.editorNote: Perfect for that friend who paid for an annual gym pass and went twice — maybe keep your distance after sending
variations: Step one to losing weight is not gaining weight. Those who already gained need to go the other way.
People who run cover more distance than people who don't. That's called exercise.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學上班通勤主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
通勤研究發現：有去上班的人，通常人會在公司。

沒去的，就不在。

至於請假在家但是滿腦子工作的人，他們的身體在家，靈魂正在加班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上傳到同事群組，沒有人會反對你的論點，因為這完全是事實
variations: 捷運上有人的時候，車廂裡的人會比較多。沒有人的時候，剛好相反。
出門上班的第一步，就是要先出門。沒出門的，今天可能就到不了公司。

en.content:
Commute studies have confirmed: people who go to work are usually at the office.

Those who don't go are not.

As for the ones who took the day off but can't stop thinking about work, their body is home and their soul is doing overtime.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the team chat on Monday morning — nobody can argue, it's just facts
variations: When there are people on the subway, the train is more crowded. When there aren't, it's the opposite.
Step one of going to work is leaving the house. Skip that step and you probably won't make it in.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學手機電量主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
工程師指出：手機有電的時候，可以使用；沒電的時候，使用上會比較困難。

至於充電中的手機，目前正在充電。

充飽之後，就變成有電的手機了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直問「我手機怎麼變這麼慢」、結果發現只是電量剩 3% 的朋友
variations: 想延長手機電量的方法，就是不要用手機。不用的話，電量比較不會減少。
充電線插上去，手機才會充到電。沒插的話，目前還在原本的電量。

en.content:
Engineers point out: when a phone has battery, you can use it; when it doesn't, using it becomes considerably harder.

As for a phone that's charging, it is currently charging.

Once fully charged, it becomes a phone with battery.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend constantly asking 'why is my phone so slow?' only to realize they're at 3% battery
variations: The best way to extend your phone's battery is to not use it. Not using it tends to keep the battery from dropping.
Plugging in the charger is what makes the phone charge. Without that, it remains at its current battery level.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學購物網購主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
消費行為專家表示：有買東西的人，戶頭裡通常會少一些錢。

沒買的，數字會比較穩定。

至於那些「只是看看」結果結帳的人，他們確實看了，後來也買了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 雙十一隔天早上傳給整夜在搶優惠的朋友，他會在收件匣裡安靜地接受這個事實
variations: 加入購物車的東西，要按結帳才會變成你的。沒按的話，目前還是別人家的庫存。
免運門檻達到的方法，就是再多買一點。多買的，剛好就過了門檻。

en.content:
Consumer behavior experts confirm: people who buy things usually have less money in their accounts.

Those who don't buy keep more stable numbers.

As for those who 'just wanted to look' and ended up checking out — they did look, and they did buy.
en.editorNote: Send this to a friend the morning after a midnight sale — they'll quietly accept the truth from inside their inbox of order confirmations
variations: Items in your cart only become yours after checkout. Without checkout, they remain someone else's inventory.
The way to hit free-shipping threshold is to buy more. Buying more is exactly what gets you over the threshold.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學記憶遺忘主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
心理學家發現：記得的事情，目前還記得；忘記的事情，已經想不起來了。

至於那些「我好像記得，但又有點忘了」的事情，它們正處於記得和忘記之間。

等想起來的時候，就回到了記得的那一邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個進廚房之後忘記自己是來幹嘛的朋友，他會站在原地三秒鐘消化這段話
variations: 想記住一件事的方法，就是不要忘記。忘記的話，就需要重新記一次。
回憶起來的事，原本就記得，只是剛剛沒想到而已。

en.content:
Psychologists have found: things you remember, you currently remember; things you've forgotten, you can no longer recall.

As for the 'I think I remember but kind of forgot' category, they exist somewhere between remembered and forgotten.

The moment they come back, they return to the remembered side.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who walks into the kitchen and immediately forgets why — they'll stand still for three seconds processing this
variations: The way to remember something is to not forget it. Forgetting it means you'll have to memorize it again.
Things you suddenly recall were already remembered — you just hadn't thought of them yet.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學週末假日主題重新創作

zh-tw.content:
行事曆研究指出：週末過完之後，下週就要開始了。

還沒過完的話，目前還在週末。

至於那種「週末感覺一下就沒了」的人，他們的週末，跟其他人一樣是兩天，只是過得比較快。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上九點傳給朋友，沒有任何安慰效果，但他會跟你一起靜靜地接受明天就是禮拜一
variations: 禮拜五下班之後，就是週末了。還沒下班的話，目前還在禮拜五。
放假的時候不用上班；不放假的時候，那就要上班。

en.content:
Calendar studies indicate: once the weekend ends, next week begins.

If it hasn't ended, you're still in the weekend.

As for those who feel like 'the weekend disappeared instantly,' their weekend is the same two days as everyone else's — it just went by faster.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend at 9pm Sunday — offers zero comfort, but you'll both quietly accept that tomorrow is Monday together
variations: Once Friday work ends, the weekend begins. If you haven't clocked out, it's still Friday.
On days off, you don't work; on days that aren't off, you do.

items.sourceNote: 「俗話說得好」格式重新創作，加入錢的主題

zh-tw.content:
經濟學家研究指出：一個人之所以沒錢，最主要的原因是他帳戶裡的錢不夠多。

如果帳戶裡的錢夠多，那他就會變成一個有錢的人。

至於那種「明明有錢卻說自己沒錢」的人，他們其實是有錢的，只是嘴巴上沒錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在被朋友問「最近怎麼那麼窮」的時候原句奉還，對方會沉默三秒然後不知道該怎麼接
variations: 想變有錢的方法很簡單，只要你有錢就可以了。
錢花掉之後就沒了，沒花掉的還在。這是個人理財的基本原理。

en.content:
Economists have determined: the primary reason a person has no money is that there is not enough money in their bank account.

If there were enough money in the account, they would become a person with money.

As for those who 'have money but claim to be broke,' they actually do have money — they just verbally don't.
en.editorNote: Use this when a friend asks 'why are you so broke lately' — they'll go silent for three seconds trying to figure out how to respond
variations: The secret to becoming rich is simple: just have money.
Once money is spent, it's gone. Money not spent is still there. This is the foundation of personal finance.

items.sourceNote: 睡眠主題廢話文學重新創作

zh-tw.content:
睡眠專家提醒：睡著之後就會進入睡眠狀態，醒過來就不睡了。

如果你還沒睡著，那就表示你目前是醒的。

至於那種「躺在床上滑手機到天亮」的人，他們其實沒有失眠，只是沒在睡覺而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨三點傳給也還沒睡的朋友，兩個人會一起靜靜地承認彼此都是自願的
variations: 閉上眼睛之後，你就看不到東西了；睜開眼睛，視野就會恢復。
你睡了幾個小時，醒來之後就是過了幾個小時。

en.content:
Sleep experts remind us: once you fall asleep, you enter a state of sleep; once you wake up, you are no longer sleeping.

If you haven't fallen asleep yet, that means you are currently awake.

As for those who 'lie in bed scrolling on their phone until dawn,' they don't actually have insomnia — they're just not sleeping.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's also still up at 3am — you'll both quietly acknowledge that you chose this
variations: When you close your eyes, you can't see things. When you open them, your vision returns.
However many hours you slept, that's how many hours have passed when you wake up.

items.sourceNote: 「越過山」格式參考，改寫為健身運動主題

zh-tw.content:
運動科學最新發現：當你開始運動的那一刻，你就已經在運動了。

停下來之後，運動就結束了。

所以想要持續運動的人，只要不停下來，就可以一直運動下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在健身房裡傳給跟你一起辦會員卻已經半年沒去的朋友，喚醒他內心對運動的麻木
variations: 減肥的原理很簡單：當你變瘦的時候，你就減肥成功了。
跑步的人會比走路的人快，除非那個走路的人走得比較快。

en.content:
The latest in exercise science: the moment you start exercising, you are already exercising.

Once you stop, the exercise is over.

So for those who want to exercise consistently, just don't stop — and you can keep exercising indefinitely.
en.editorNote: Send this to your gym buddy who signed up six months ago and hasn't been since — gently reawaken their numbed athletic spirit
variations: The principle of weight loss is simple: once you become thinner, you have successfully lost weight.
Runners are faster than walkers, unless the walker happens to be walking faster.

items.sourceNote: 聊天回覆主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
通訊軟體研究指出：對方已讀不回，代表他看到了但沒回。

如果他回了，那就不叫已讀不回。

至於那種「已讀很久才回」的情況，他其實有回，只是回得比較晚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合用來安慰被已讀不回的朋友，雖然講完之後對方還是沒被安慰到，但至少你們可以一起困惑
variations: 他傳訊息給你，就表示他想跟你說話；他沒傳，就表示他現在沒在傳。
對話框顯示「對方正在輸入」，代表對方正在打字；沒顯示的話，代表沒在打。

en.content:
Messaging app research indicates: when someone leaves you on read, it means they saw it but didn't reply.

If they had replied, it wouldn't be called being left on read.

As for those who 'reply after a really long time,' they did actually reply — just later.
en.editorNote: Use this to comfort a friend who got left on read — they won't actually feel better, but at least you can both be confused together
variations: When someone texts you, it means they want to talk to you. When they don't, it means they're currently not texting.
When the chat shows 'typing...', it means the other person is typing. When it doesn't show that, they're not.

items.sourceNote: 外送主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
外送平台統計顯示：你點的餐送到之前，是還沒送到的狀態。

送到之後，就送到了。

至於那種「我餐到底什麼時候會到」的情況，答案是：送到的時候就會到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 等外送等到崩潰時可以默念一遍，能讓你瞬間從焦慮轉為一種看破紅塵的平靜
variations: 你點的食物吃完之後就沒了，沒吃完的話還在。
外送員到了的時候會通知你，沒通知就表示還沒到。

en.content:
Food delivery app statistics show: before your order arrives, it is in a state of not having arrived.

Once it arrives, it has arrived.

As for the question 'when will my food actually get here,' the answer is: it will get here when it arrives.
en.editorNote: Recite this silently while waiting for a delivery — it'll instantly shift you from anxiety into a kind of zen acceptance of the universe
variations: Once you finish your food, it's gone. If you haven't finished it, it's still there.
The delivery driver will notify you when they arrive. If you haven't been notified, they haven't arrived yet.

items.sourceNote: 職場主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
人力資源研究指出：當你的主管走過來說「方便聊一下嗎」，他就是要找你聊一下。

如果你說不方便，他還是會找你聊。

所以這個問題的選項只有一個，就是聊一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在週一早上傳到同事群組，提前讓大家做好心理建設
variations: 開會的時候主管說「我長話短說」，通常代表他要講很久。
下班時間到了之後，如果你還在公司，那你就是在加班；如果你回家了，那就沒有。

en.content:
HR research has shown: when your manager walks over and says 'got a minute to chat?', he wants to chat with you.

If you say no, he will still chat with you.

So this question really only has one valid answer, which is yes.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the team chat on a Monday morning to mentally prepare everyone for the week ahead
variations: When your boss says 'I'll keep this short' in a meeting, it usually means it will be long.
If you're still at the office after your shift ends, you're working overtime. If you went home, you're not.

items.sourceNote: 睡眠主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
睡眠科學最新研究：當你睡著了之後，你就睡著了。

如果你還醒著，那就還沒睡著。

至於失眠的人，他們的特徵是想睡但睡不著，這跟睡著的人剛好相反。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨三點失眠時可以默念這段，讓你從焦慮升級成困惑，然後不知不覺就天亮了
variations: 早睡的人，比晚睡的人，睡得早。這是科學。
想睡覺的時候去睡，就比較容易睡著；不想睡的時候硬睡，就比較難睡著。

en.content:
The latest in sleep science: once you fall asleep, you are asleep.

If you're still awake, you haven't fallen asleep yet.

As for people with insomnia, they are characterized by wanting to sleep but being unable to — the exact opposite of people who are asleep.
en.editorNote: Recite this silently at 3am when you can't sleep — it elevates anxiety into confusion, and before you know it, the sun is up
variations: People who go to bed early sleep earlier than people who go to bed late. This is science.
Sleeping when you want to sleep is easier. Forcing sleep when you don't want to is harder.

items.sourceNote: 金錢主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
經濟學家發現：當你把錢花掉之後，那筆錢就不在你的戶頭裡了。

如果你沒花，它就還在。

所以月底沒錢的人，通常是因為他們把錢花掉了，這個現象在每個月底都會準時出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每個月25號之後傳到朋友群組，搭配一張哭臉貼圖效果加倍
variations: 薪水入帳的當下，戶頭會多一筆錢；扣掉房租、卡費、外送之後，就會變回原本的數字。
存錢的原理很簡單：當你不把錢花掉的時候，那筆錢就被存起來了。

en.content:
Economists have discovered: once you spend money, that money is no longer in your account.

If you don't spend it, it's still there.

This is why people who are broke at the end of the month are usually broke because they spent their money — a phenomenon that occurs reliably on schedule every month.
en.editorNote: Send this to the group chat after the 25th, paired with a crying emoji for maximum impact
variations: The moment your paycheck hits, your balance goes up. After rent, credit cards, and delivery orders, it returns to its original number.
The principle of saving is simple: when you don't spend money, that money is saved.

items.sourceNote: 旅行主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
旅遊專家指出：出門旅行之後，你就不在家了。

回到家以後，你又會在家了。

至於那些「旅行回來反而更累」的人，他們的疲勞主要來自於旅行，這個現象目前沒有解方，建議再排一次旅行。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放假結束的週日晚上傳給朋友，讓人體會到「假期收假」的宇宙真理
variations: 去旅行之前要先打包行李，因為不打包的話，你就沒有行李可以帶。
搭飛機的人，到了目的地通常會下飛機；沒下飛機的人，可能還在飛機上。

en.content:
Travel experts have noted: once you leave for a trip, you are no longer home.

When you come back, you'll be home again.

As for people who feel even more tired after a vacation, the tiredness mainly comes from the trip itself. There is currently no known solution. The recommendation is to plan another trip.
en.editorNote: Best sent to friends on a Sunday night at the end of a long weekend — they'll feel the cosmic truth of vacation ending
variations: Before going on a trip, you need to pack. Without packing, you have no luggage to bring.
People who take flights usually get off the plane at their destination. Those who didn't get off may still be on the plane.

items.sourceNote: 學習主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
教育研究顯示：考試之前複習的人，通常比沒複習的人準備得多。

沒複習的人，準備得比較少。

至於那種「明明有念書還是考不好」的情況，可能是因為他念的剛好不是考試會考的，這個現象在每次考試都會出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合考前一週傳給臨時抱佛腳的朋友，讓他在崩潰之前先笑一下
variations: 讀書讀到睡著的人，醒來之後通常會發現自己睡著了。
背過的單字，在考試的時候想得起來就考得出來；想不起來就考不出來。

en.content:
Educational research shows: people who review before an exam are generally more prepared than those who don't.

Those who don't review are less prepared.

As for the situation where 'I studied and still failed,' it may be that what you studied just happened not to be on the test. This phenomenon occurs in every exam.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend cramming the week before an exam — give them one laugh before the full breakdown
variations: Students who fall asleep while studying usually realize they fell asleep when they wake up.
Vocabulary you memorized can be recalled on the test if you can remember it. If you can't remember it, you can't recall it.

items.sourceNote: 咖啡因主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
營養師指出：喝了咖啡的人，通常比沒喝咖啡的人多喝了一杯咖啡。

沒喝咖啡的人，少喝了一杯咖啡。

至於那種「我今天喝了三杯還是想睡」的情況，研究顯示，主要原因是你昨天沒睡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合星期一早上傳到辦公室群組，搭配第二杯美式特別好用
variations: 咖啡因會讓人變得清醒，是因為它本來就是讓人清醒的東西。
拿鐵裡面有牛奶，所以喝起來有牛奶味；美式沒有，所以喝起來沒有。

en.content:
Nutritionists report: people who drink coffee have generally consumed one more cup of coffee than people who haven't.

Those who haven't had coffee have consumed one less cup.

As for the situation of 'I had three cups today and I'm still sleepy,' research indicates the main reason is that you didn't sleep last night.
en.editorNote: Best deployed to the office chat on Monday morning, paired with a photo of your second americano
variations: Caffeine makes you alert because it is, by definition, the thing that makes you alert.
Lattes contain milk, which is why they taste like milk. Americanos don't, which is why they don't.

items.sourceNote: AI 工具主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
科技專家表示：用 AI 寫的文章，通常是 AI 寫的。

自己寫的文章，通常不是 AI 寫的。

至於那種「看起來像 AI 寫的人類文章」與「看起來像人類寫的 AI 文章」，目前仍在交互驗證中，預計永遠不會有結論。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合丟到內容創作者的群組，順便引發一場誰是真人的存在主義危機
variations: 你問 AI 一個問題，它就會回答你；你不問，它就不會回答。
AI 不會累，是因為它本來就不會累。

en.content:
Tech experts confirm: articles written by AI are typically written by AI.

Articles you wrote yourself are typically not written by AI.

As for 'human-written content that looks AI-generated' versus 'AI content that looks human-written,' the cross-verification is ongoing and a conclusion is not expected to ever be reached.
en.editorNote: Drop this in a creator group chat to trigger a healthy existential crisis about authorship
variations: If you ask an AI a question, it answers. If you don't ask, it doesn't.
AI doesn't get tired because it was never the kind of thing that gets tired.

items.sourceNote: 夏天冷氣主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
氣象專家指出：開了冷氣的房間，通常會比沒開冷氣的房間涼。

沒開冷氣的房間，會比開了的還熱。

至於那種「開冷氣電費很貴，不開冷氣會中暑」的兩難，目前唯一解方是去便利商店站著看雜誌，順便享受店家的冷氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在電費單寄到當週傳給朋友，順便配上一張融化的貼圖
variations: 夏天會熱，是因為夏天本來就是熱的季節。
冷氣調 26 度，房間就會慢慢變成 26 度；不開，就不會。

en.content:
Meteorologists report: rooms with the AC on are usually cooler than rooms without.

Rooms without AC are usually hotter than rooms with.

As for the dilemma of 'AC is expensive, no AC is heatstroke,' the only known solution is to stand inside a convenience store pretending to read magazines while enjoying their air conditioning for free.
en.editorNote: Send the week your electric bill arrives, ideally paired with a melting emoji
variations: Summer is hot because summer is, by nature, the hot season.
Set the AC to 78 and the room slowly becomes 78. Leave it off, and it doesn't.

items.sourceNote: 交友軟體主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
社會學家觀察到：在交友軟體上配對成功的人，通常有配對到對象。

沒配對到的人，就沒有對象。

至於那種「配對到了但對方不回訊息」的情況，研究顯示，他不是不會回，是不想回。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在你朋友又被已讀不回的那個晚上傳給他，記得搭配一杯酒
variations: 右滑的人，比左滑的人更想認識對方。
你不發訊息，對方就不會收到；你發了，對方收不收得到要看他開不開 App。

en.content:
Sociologists have observed: people who match on dating apps generally have a match.

People without a match do not.

As for the case of 'we matched but they don't reply,' studies indicate it's not that they can't reply — it's that they don't want to.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend on the night they got left on read again, ideally with a drink emoji attached
variations: People who swipe right are more interested in meeting the other person than people who swipe left.
If you don't send a message, they won't receive one. If you do, whether they see it depends on whether they open the app.

items.sourceNote: 週日夜晚焦慮主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
心理學家發現：週日晚上會焦慮的人，通常是因為隔天是星期一。

如果隔天不是星期一，他們就不會在星期天晚上焦慮，而是換一天焦慮。

至於那種「我都還沒上班就累了」的感覺，是真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在週日晚上九點傳給準備收假的朋友，比晚安貼圖還有效
variations: 星期一會來，是因為星期天會過去。
放假的時候不想上班；上班的時候不想上班。這兩件事不衝突。

en.content:
Psychologists have found: people who get anxious on Sunday night are anxious because the next day is Monday.

If the next day weren't Monday, they wouldn't be anxious on Sunday night — they'd be anxious on a different day.

As for the feeling of 'I'm tired before I've even started work,' that part is real.
en.editorNote: Send at 9pm on a Sunday to anyone bracing for the week — more effective than a goodnight sticker
variations: Monday arrives because Sunday ends.
You don't want to work on the weekend; you don't want to work on weekdays either. These are not in conflict.

items.sourceNote: 咖啡因主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
營養學家指出：喝了咖啡的人，通常攝取了咖啡因。

沒喝咖啡的人，就沒有攝取到。

至於那種「喝了還是很累」的情況，研究顯示，你需要的不是咖啡，是離職。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在週一早上傳到辦公室群組，記得搭配一個馬克杯貼圖
variations: 想睡的時候喝咖啡會比較清醒；不想睡的時候喝，也會比較清醒。
一杯咖啡喝完之後，杯子裡通常就沒有咖啡了。這是已知的物理現象。

en.content:
Nutritionists point out: people who drink coffee generally consume caffeine.

People who don't drink coffee do not.

As for the case of 'I drank it and I'm still tired,' studies indicate what you need isn't coffee — it's a resignation letter.
en.editorNote: Send to the office group chat on Monday morning, ideally with a mug emoji
variations: Coffee makes you more alert when you're sleepy. It also makes you more alert when you're not.
Once a cup of coffee is finished, the cup generally contains no coffee. This is a known law of physics.

items.sourceNote: 健身減肥主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
運動科學家發現：有去健身房的人，比沒去的人更常出現在健身房。

沒去的人，通常出現在沙發上。

至於那種「辦了會員卡卻沒去」的情況，數據顯示，你買的不是健身，是贖罪券。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給辦了年費會員卻只去過兩次的朋友，效果拔群
