variations: 想瘦的人只要少吃多動就會瘦；做不到的人，就是現在這個樣子。
體重計上的數字，是你站上去的時候才會出現的。你不站，它就不知道。

en.content:
Exercise scientists have found: people who go to the gym appear at the gym more often than people who don't.

Those who don't go are usually located on the couch.

As for 'I bought a membership but never went,' the data suggests you didn't buy fitness — you bought an indulgence.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who got an annual membership and went twice — devastatingly effective
variations: People who want to lose weight just need to eat less and move more. People who can't are shaped exactly the way they are now.
The number on the scale only appears when you step on it. If you don't step on it, it doesn't know either.

items.sourceNote: 網購主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
經濟學家觀察到：在網路上下單的人，通常會收到包裹。

沒下單的人，就不會收到。

至於那種「買的時候很開心，刷卡帳單來的時候很痛苦」的循環，研究顯示，這兩種感覺都是真的，只是時間差。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在大促銷的隔天傳給剛剁手的朋友，搭配一個哭笑不得的表情
variations: 加入購物車的東西，要結帳才會變成你的；不結帳的話，它還是商店的。
免運門檻湊到的時候會省到運費，但你會多花一個你本來不需要的東西的錢。

en.content:
Economists have observed: people who place orders online generally receive packages.

People who don't place orders do not.

As for the cycle of 'so happy when buying, so miserable when the credit card bill arrives,' research confirms both feelings are real — they just happen at different times.
en.editorNote: Send the day after a big sale to a friend who just maxed out their cart, with a crying-laughing face
variations: Items in your cart only become yours after checkout. If you don't check out, they still belong to the store.
When you hit the free-shipping threshold, you save on shipping — by spending money on something you didn't need.

items.sourceNote: 熬夜睡眠主題廢話文學新創

zh-tw.content:
睡眠專家發現：早睡的人，通常比晚睡的人更早睡著。

晚睡的人，則睡得比較晚。

至於那種「明明很累卻一直滑手機不睡」的行為，研究稱之為報復性熬夜，意思是你在報復一個讓你沒時間的人，而那個人就是你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在凌晨一點傳給還在線上的朋友，提醒他你們都該睡了卻都沒睡
variations: 睡飽的人隔天通常比較有精神；沒睡飽的人，隔天就用咖啡硬撐。
你說再滑五分鐘就睡，這句話本身需要再多滑兩個小時來驗證。

en.content:
Sleep experts have found: people who sleep early generally fall asleep earlier than people who sleep late.

People who sleep late fall asleep later.

As for 'I'm exhausted but I keep scrolling instead of sleeping,' researchers call this revenge bedtime procrastination — meaning you're getting revenge on the person who left you no free time, and that person is you.
en.editorNote: Send at 1am to a friend who's still online, reminding you both you should be asleep and neither of you is
variations: People who get enough sleep are usually more energetic the next day. People who don't power through on coffee.
You said you'd sleep after five more minutes. Verifying that statement requires two more hours of scrolling.

items.sourceNote: 套套邏輯廢話文學新創，參考經典格式

zh-tw.content:
俗話說得好：俗話說得好。

至於是哪句俗話，研究指出，只要它流傳得夠久，就會被稱為俗話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在群組裡有人引用名言時接話，瞬間讓對話原地打轉
variations: 古人說的話，通常都是古人說的。這一點目前還沒有反例。

en.content:
As the old saying goes: the old saying goes.

Which saying exactly? Research indicates that as long as it's been around long enough, it qualifies as old.
en.editorNote: Use this right after someone drops a quote in a group chat — it sends the conversation spinning in place
variations: Things wise people said were, in fact, said by wise people. No counterexamples have been found.

items.sourceNote: 聽君一席話經典變體，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
聽你講了一個小時，我感覺就像聽了一個小時。

資訊量這種東西，有就是有，沒有也看不出來少了什麼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合朋友長篇大論講完之後，假裝很有收穫地回他
variations: 你這番話讓我恍然大悟，原來我剛剛什麼都沒聽懂。

en.content:
I listened to you talk for an hour, and it felt exactly like listening for an hour.

As for information content — when it's there it's there, and when it's not, you can't tell anything's missing.
en.editorNote: Use after a friend finishes a long-winded explanation, while pretending you got a lot out of it
variations: Your speech was a real eye-opener — it opened my eyes to the fact that I understood none of it.

items.sourceNote: technically true 風格廢話，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
惡夢算是夢嗎？

還是說，那是一部你自己編劇、自己導演、自己主演的免費恐怖片？

專家表示：兩種說法目前都成立。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合早上傳給昨晚說自己做惡夢的朋友，順便嚇他一下
variations: 你睡覺時閉著眼睛，是為了看夢；醒著時睜開眼睛，是為了看帳單。

en.content:
Are nightmares dreams?

Or are they free horror movies that you write, direct, and star in?

Experts confirm: both descriptions currently hold.
en.editorNote: Send in the morning to a friend who mentioned a bad dream — bonus points for the mild dread
variations: You close your eyes when you sleep to watch dreams, and open them when you wake to watch your bills.

items.sourceNote: 身體哲學 technically true 風格新創

zh-tw.content:
你的身體裡有一副骨頭嗎？

還是說，是你（也就是你的大腦）住在一副骨頭裡面？

這個問題科學家想了很久，結論是：以上皆是。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合半夜睡不著時傳到群組，讓大家一起睡不著
variations: 你不是在用手機，是手機在用你的兩根大拇指。差別只在誰先放下誰。

en.content:
Do you have a skeleton inside you?

Or are you — meaning your brain — living inside a skeleton?

Scientists have pondered this at length and concluded: all of the above.
en.editorNote: Drop this in a group chat at midnight so nobody else can fall asleep either
variations: You're not using your phone — your phone is using your two thumbs. The only question is who puts the other down first.

items.sourceNote: 時間流逝廢話文學經典變體，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
好消息是，過完了五月就到六月了。

壞消息是，過完了六月就到七月了。

日曆專家提醒：這個流程目前沒有暫停鍵。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合月初傳到工作群組，提醒大家又要過一個月了
variations: 週一過完就是週二，週二過完就是週三，至於週末，它過完之後就又是週一了。

en.content:
Good news: once May is over, it'll be June.

Bad news: once June is over, it'll be July.

Calendar experts note that this process currently has no pause button.
en.editorNote: Send to the work group chat at the start of a month, reminding everyone another one's begun
variations: After Monday comes Tuesday, after Tuesday comes Wednesday, and as for the weekend — once it's over, it's Monday again.

items.sourceNote: 「我知道你很急，但你先別急」廢話文學經典變體，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我知道你現在很忙，但你可以先不要忙。

等你不忙的時候，自然就有空了。

心理學家表示，這個說法雖然沒有幫到你，但確實沒有錯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給整天喊忙的同事或朋友，看他氣到沒空回你
variations: 我知道你很累，但你累的時候休息一下，就會比較不累。

en.content:
I know you're really busy right now, but you could just not be busy.

Whenever you stop being busy, you'll naturally have free time.

Psychologists confirm this advice doesn't help you, but it isn't wrong either.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's always complaining they're swamped, then watch them get too annoyed to reply
variations: I know you're tired, but if you rest when you're tired, you'll end up less tired.

items.sourceNote: 「you can drink cement, but only once」technically true 風格，改寫為廢話文學

zh-tw.content:
任何東西你都可以嘗試吃一次。

至於能不能吃第二次，那要看你第一次吃完還在不在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種什麼都想嘗鮮、看到新口味就要試的朋友
variations: 每件危險的事你都做得到，只是有些事你只做得到一次。

en.content:
You can try eating absolutely anything once.

Whether you get a second try depends entirely on whether you're still around after the first.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who has to taste every new flavor and tries everything at least once
variations: You're capable of doing any dangerous thing — it's just that some of them you can only do one time.

items.sourceNote: 雞是恐龍的 technically true 觀察，改寫為廢話文學

zh-tw.content:
科學家說，雞其實是一種恐龍。

所以你今天午餐吃的，嚴格來說，是一隻體型縮水、運氣也縮水的恐龍。

這個事實對雞來說沒有任何好處。
zh-tw.editorNote: 吃炸雞的時候傳到群組，讓大家邊吃邊陷入沉思
variations: 鯨魚是哺乳類，所以海裡最大的動物，技術上跟你一樣都得換氣。

en.content:
Scientists say a chicken is, in fact, a kind of dinosaur.

So what you had for lunch was, strictly speaking, a dinosaur that shrank in both size and luck.

This fact does the chicken no favors whatsoever.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat while everyone's eating fried chicken so they chew and contemplate at the same time
variations: A whale is a mammal, so the biggest animal in the ocean technically has to come up for air, just like you.

items.sourceNote: 「我第一次去美國驚呆了，那麼多美國人」廢話文學風格，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我第一次去海邊的時候嚇了一跳，

那裡居然有那麼多的海水。

而且每一滴，據說都是濕的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 出去玩打卡時配這段文案，朋友會懷疑你是不是中暑了
variations: 我第一次去夜市才發現，原來夜市裡賣的東西，幾乎全部都是可以買的。

en.content:
The first time I went to the beach, I was stunned —

there was an unbelievable amount of seawater there.

And every single drop of it, I'm told, was wet.
en.editorNote: Pair this with a vacation photo and your friends will wonder if you got heatstroke
variations: First time I went to a night market, I realized nearly everything they sell there is, in fact, available for purchase.

items.sourceNote: 「情況就是這麼個情況，具體什麼情況還得看情況」廢話文學經典，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
問題就是這個問題，至於是什麼問題，

要等我們搞清楚問題之後才知道是什麼問題。

在那之前，問題還是個問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 開會被問「現在到底什麼狀況」時，可以照念，氣氛保證凝結
variations: 計畫是有的，至於計畫是什麼，等計畫出來你就知道了。

en.content:
The problem is the problem. As for what the problem actually is,

we'll only know what the problem is once we've figured out the problem.

Until then, the problem remains a problem.
en.editorNote: Recite this verbatim in a meeting when someone asks what the situation is — the room will freeze
variations: There is a plan. As for what the plan is, you'll find out once the plan is out.

items.sourceNote: 「人們說沒有事情是不可能的，但我每天都在無所事事」廢話文學風格，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
大家都說「天下無難事」，

可是我每天都在做沒有事做的事。

所以嚴格來說，我也算是把事情做完了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被長輩問「最近在忙什麼」時的最佳回覆，既誠實又無懈可擊
variations: 別人說「只要肯努力就會成功」，而我每天都很努力地什麼都不做。

en.content:
Everyone says nothing is impossible,

yet I manage to do nothing every single day.

So technically, I've already accomplished the impossible.
en.editorNote: The ideal reply when a relative asks what you've been up to — honest and airtight
variations: They say success comes to those who try, and I try very hard to do absolutely nothing.

items.sourceNote: 「閉上眼睛就看不見」廢話文學風格，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我發現一件很神奇的事——

只要我一閉上眼睛，眼前的世界就會變成一片漆黑。

而且只要再睜開，它又回來了，從不缺席。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很適合在朋友炫耀什麼冷知識時，用更冷的回他
variations: 我研究很久才確定，原來人只要把耳朵摀起來，聽到的聲音就會變小。

en.content:
I discovered something remarkable —

the moment I close my eyes, the world in front of me goes completely dark.

And the moment I open them again, it returns, never once failing to show up.
en.editorNote: Great for out-cooling a friend who's bragging about some random fun fact
variations: After much research I can confirm: covering your ears makes the sounds you hear noticeably quieter.

items.sourceNote: 「世界上有三種人：會數數的和不會數數的」廢話文學風格，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
這世界上只有兩種人：

一種是看得懂這句話的人，

另一種，是看完還在等第三種的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 丟在群組裡，會有人認真往下滑找第三種，屢試不爽
variations: 人可以分成兩類：分得出自己是哪一類的，和正在懷疑自己是不是兩類都算的。

en.content:
There are only two kinds of people in this world:

those who understand this sentence,

and those still scrolling down, waiting for the third kind.
en.editorNote: Post it in a group chat and watch someone scroll, genuinely looking for the third kind
variations: People fall into two categories: those who can tell which category they're in, and those wondering if they count as both.

items.sourceNote: 「據我所知，我一無所知」廢話文學經典，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
就我所掌握的資訊來看，

我目前掌握的資訊非常有限。

至於有多有限，這我也不太清楚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 開會被問到不熟的議題時，照念一遍，沒人能挑出毛病
variations: 根據我多年的觀察，我觀察到的事情其實沒幾件。

en.content:
Based on everything I currently know,

what I currently know is extremely limited.

Exactly how limited, I'm afraid I'm not sure.
en.editorNote: Recite this when a meeting lands on a topic you know nothing about — nobody can argue with it
variations: After years of careful observation, I've observed that I've observed very little.

items.sourceNote: 「灰姑娘的鞋如果合腳，為什麼會掉」廢話文學風格，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
如果那雙鞋真的那麼合腳，

那它為什麼會在半路上掉下來？

所以結論是：合腳的鞋，有時候也沒那麼合腳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在朋友講一個聽起來很有道理、其實毫無道理的話時接這句
variations: 如果雨傘真的能擋雨，那為什麼下大雨的時候我的鞋還是濕的？這值得深思。

en.content:
If that shoe really fit so perfectly,

then why did it slip off halfway home?

Conclusion: a perfectly fitting shoe sometimes doesn't fit perfectly after all.
en.editorNote: Perfect comeback when a friend says something that sounds logical but completely isn't
variations: If umbrellas really keep out the rain, why are my shoes still wet in a downpour? Something to ponder.

items.sourceNote: 「我知道你很急，但你先別急」廢話文學經典，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我知道你現在很急，

但這件事急也沒用，所以你先別急。

等到可以急的時候，我再通知你急。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友在群組裡狂催進度時，丟這句保證讓對方更急
variations: 你先冷靜一下，等冷靜完了我們再來討論為什麼你需要冷靜。

en.content:
I know you're in a hurry right now,

but hurrying won't help, so please don't hurry.

When it's actually time to hurry, I'll let you know to hurry.
en.editorNote: Drop this when someone is frantically chasing you for an update — guaranteed to make them more frantic
variations: Calm down first, and once you've calmed down we can discuss why you needed to calm down.

items.sourceNote: 「如果不成功，就有失敗的風險」套套邏輯名言，廢話文學風格重新創作

zh-tw.content:
專家提醒：如果這次沒有成功，

那麼這次就有很高的機率失敗。

所以只要不失敗，基本上就算成功了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當作報告結尾的「總結」，聽起來很有道理但其實什麼都沒講
variations: 只要不放棄，你就還沒放棄。這是堅持下去最關鍵的一步。

en.content:
Experts warn: if this attempt does not succeed,

then there is a very high chance it will fail.

So as long as you don't fail, you've basically succeeded.
en.editorNote: Use it to wrap up a presentation — sounds like a solid conclusion while saying nothing at all
variations: As long as you don't give up, you haven't given up. That's the most crucial step to keeping going.

items.sourceNote: Yogi Berra「總是在你最後找的地方找到」廢話文學風格重新創作

zh-tw.content:
東西永遠都是在你最後找的那個地方找到的。

因為找到之後，你就不會再繼續找了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 幫人找東西找到的時候講這句，對方會愣三秒
variations: 你錢包最後一定會出現在某個地方，而那個地方就是它出現的地方。

en.content:
You always find it in the last place you look.

Because once you've found it, you stop looking.
en.editorNote: Say this right after helping someone find their keys — watch them freeze for three seconds
variations: Your wallet will eventually turn up somewhere, and that somewhere is exactly where it turns up.

items.sourceNote: 「我第一次去美國，沒見過那麼多美國人」廢話文學經典，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我第一次去日本的時候真的被震撼到，

我從來沒有在一個國家裡，看過那麼多日本人。

滿街都是，一個比一個還日本。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友分享出國旅遊照片時，認真地回這句，效果拔群
variations: 我去了一趟海邊，發現那裡的海水竟然全部都是海水，一滴都不馬虎。

en.content:
My first trip to Japan completely blew my mind.

I had never seen so many Japanese people in a single country before.

They were everywhere — each one more Japanese than the last.
en.editorNote: Reply with this, totally straight-faced, when a friend posts their vacation photos
variations: I went to the beach and discovered the water there was entirely made of seawater — not a single drop off-brand.

items.sourceNote: Yogi Berra「不管你去哪，你都在那裡」廢話文學風格重新創作

zh-tw.content:
不管你走到哪裡，

你都會在你所在的地方。

這也是為什麼，你永遠擺脫不了你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 假掰一點念出來，很適合配上夕陽照當限動文案
variations: 無論你決定做什麼，你最後做的都會是你做的那件事。

en.content:
No matter where you go,

you will be wherever you are.

Which is exactly why you can never escape yourself.
en.editorNote: Read it with fake gravitas — pairs perfectly with a sunset photo for your story caption
variations: Whatever you decide to do, the thing you end up doing will be the thing you did.

items.sourceNote: 「我知道你很急，但你先別急」廢話文學風格重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我知道你現在很著急，

但你先別著急。

等你不急了，這件事就不急了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友在群組裡催進度的時候丟這句，保證對方更急
variations: 你先冷靜一下，等你冷靜下來，你就冷靜了。

en.content:
I can tell you're in a hurry right now,

but try not to be in a hurry.

Once you stop being in a hurry, there will be no hurry.
en.editorNote: Send this when a friend is rushing you in chat — guaranteed to make them rush harder
variations: Just calm down. Once you've calmed down, you'll be calm.

items.sourceNote: 「據我所知，我一無所知」蘇格拉底式廢話重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
就我所知道的範圍來說，

我知道的範圍裡，

剛好就是我所知道的那些。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被問到不懂的問題時拿來打太極，對方通常會放棄追問
variations: 我能告訴你的，就是我能告訴你的那些，剩下的我就不能告訴你了。

en.content:
As far as my knowledge goes,

the things I know

are precisely the things that I happen to know.
en.editorNote: Deploy this when someone asks you a question you can't answer — they usually stop asking
variations: Everything I can tell you is everything I can tell you. The rest, I cannot tell you.

items.sourceNote: 「如果不成功，我們就有失敗的風險」循環論證式廢話重新創作

zh-tw.content:
這次如果我們沒有成功，

那我們就有很大的機率，

會面臨沒有成功的局面。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合拿來模仿主管的開會總結，假裝很有深度其實什麼都沒講
variations: 只要我們不放棄，我們就還沒有放棄。

en.content:
If we don't succeed this time,

then there's a very high chance

that we'll be facing a situation where we did not succeed.
en.editorNote: Perfect for impersonating a manager wrapping up a meeting — sounds insightful, says nothing
variations: As long as we don't give up, we technically haven't given up yet.

items.sourceNote: 「東西總是在你找到它的最後一個地方」廢話文學風格重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你弄丟的東西，

永遠都會在你最後找到它的那個地方。

找到之後，你就不會再找了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 找鑰匙找到崩潰的時候，唸給自己聽，雖然沒幫助但很真實
variations: 東西不見的時候是找不到的，找到的那一刻它就不算不見了。

en.content:
Whatever you lose

will always turn up in the very last place you look for it.

Because once you find it, you stop looking.
en.editorNote: Recite this to yourself while tearing the house apart for your keys — unhelpful, but undeniably true
variations: A missing item cannot be found while it's missing; the moment you find it, it's no longer missing.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學經典邏輯循環句改寫

zh-tw.content:
據我所知，我什麼都不知道。

而我之所以知道我什麼都不知道，

是因為我知道了這件事。

所以嚴格來說，我至少知道一件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 開會被問到不會的問題時，可以在心裡默念，外表保持鎮定
variations: 我懂的東西不多，而我懂的不多這件事，我倒是懂得很清楚。
說我不懂的人，其實也不懂我到底懂不懂。

en.content:
As far as I know, I know nothing.

And the only reason I know I know nothing

is because I know that one thing.

So technically, I do know at least one thing.
en.editorNote: Repeat this internally when you're asked a question in a meeting you have no answer to — stay calm on the outside
variations: I don't know much, but the fact that I don't know much, I know very well.
People who say I don't get it don't actually know whether I get it or not.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「我知道你很急」句式改寫

zh-tw.content:
我知道你很急，

但你先別急。

等你不急的時候，

你就會發現你其實不用急。

等你發現不用急，事情通常也辦完了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 群組裡有人在催進度、但你還沒開始做的時候，貼這個剛剛好
variations: 你越急，事情越慢；你不急了，事情還是一樣慢。
急也是一天，不急也是一天，差別在於你急的那天比較累。

en.content:
I know you're in a hurry,

but don't be in a hurry just yet.

Once you stop hurrying,

you'll realize you didn't need to hurry.

And by the time you realize that, it's usually done anyway.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat when someone's chasing you for an update you haven't started
variations: The more you rush, the slower it goes; the less you rush, it goes just as slow.
Stressing about it takes a day, not stressing takes a day too — the difference is the stressful one is more tiring.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「抛開內容不談」句式改寫

zh-tw.content:
撇開內容不談，

你講的其實還蠻有道理的。

畢竟內容才是重點，

而我們現在不談內容。
zh-tw.editorNote: 聽完一段完全聽不懂的簡報，需要回應又不想得罪人的時候
variations: 不考慮對錯的話，你說的完全正確。
如果不看結果，這個方案其實非常成功。

en.content:
Setting aside the content,

what you said actually makes a lot of sense.

Of course, the content is the whole point,

and right now we're not talking about the content.
en.editorNote: For when you just sat through a presentation you didn't understand and need to say something without offending anyone
variations: If we ignore whether it's right or wrong, you're completely correct.
If we don't look at the results, this plan was a huge success.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「明天的事後天就知道」句式改寫

zh-tw.content:
關於明天會發生什麼事，

你後天就會知道了。

如果後天還不知道，

那就再等一天。

總之到最後你一定會知道，只是時間問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友焦慮地問你明天結果會怎樣的時候，這句話保證讓他更焦慮
variations: 未來的事現在都還沒發生，所以現在擔心也是白擔心。
結果出來之前你不會知道結果，結果出來之後你就不用問了。

en.content:
As for what happens tomorrow,

you'll find out the day after.

And if you still don't know by then,

just wait one more day.

Either way you'll definitely find out — it's only a matter of time.
en.editorNote: When a friend anxiously asks how tomorrow will turn out, this is guaranteed to make them more anxious
variations: Future things haven't happened yet, so worrying about them now is wasted worry.
You won't know the result before it's out, and once it's out you don't need to ask.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「沒對象應該還是單身」句式改寫

zh-tw.content:
據觀察，沒有對象的人，

目前應該還是單身。

而一旦脫單，

他就不再是沒有對象的人了。

這個規律，至今沒有出現過例外。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被長輩問「怎麼還沒對象」的時候，用這句回敬，氣氛保證很微妙
variations: 想脫單的第一步，就是先有一個對象。
單身的人之所以單身，是因為他現在沒有在交往。

en.content:
Studies show that people without a partner

are, at present, most likely single.

And the moment they get into a relationship,

they are no longer a person without a partner.

This rule has, to this day, never had an exception.
en.editorNote: Fire this back when a relative asks why you're still single — the vibe is guaranteed to get awkward
variations: The first step to not being single is to first have a partner.
Single people are single because they're currently not dating anyone.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「俗話說」句式重新創作

zh-tw.content:
俗話說得好，

而俗話之所以說得好，

是因為它要是說得不好，

就不會變成俗話。

所以你聽到的俗話，全部都說得很好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想引用一句名言但又懶得想內容時，可以先用這句墊場
variations: 古人的智慧之所以流傳到今天，是因為沒流傳下來的我們都沒看到。
經典之所以是經典，是因為它已經被叫做經典很久了。

en.content:
As the old saying goes,

and the reason the old saying goes so well

is that if it didn't go well,

it would never have become an old saying.

So every old saying you've heard goes great.
en.editorNote: Use this as filler when you want to quote some wisdom but can't be bothered to think of any
variations: Ancient wisdom survived to today because the wisdom that didn't survive, we never got to see.
A classic is a classic because it has been called a classic for a long time.

items.sourceNote: 密碼安全 shower thought 改寫為廢話文學

zh-tw.content:
網站要求你設一個越來越複雜的密碼，

複雜到連你自己都記不住。

等到你終於忘記密碼，

他就請你重設一個新的複雜密碼。

如此一來，你的帳號就非常安全，安全到連你都進不去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每次卡在登入頁面重設密碼時，傳給同樣崩潰的朋友剛剛好
variations: 密碼設得越難記，代表你越常需要按「忘記密碼」。
最安全的帳號，就是連本人都登入不了的那一個。

en.content:
Websites make you set an ever more complicated password,

so complicated that even you can't remember it.

Then, once you finally forget it,

they kindly ask you to reset a brand new complicated password.

This way your account is extremely secure — so secure that even you can't get in.
en.editorNote: Perfect to send a fellow sufferer while you're both stuck on the reset-password page
variations: The harder your password is to remember, the more often you'll hit 'forgot password.'
The most secure account is the one even its owner can't log into.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「七日不見如隔一周」句式重新創作

zh-tw.content:
三天沒見，差不多就是隔了七十二小時。

而七十二小時，換算下來，剛好是三天。

所以我們已經三天沒見了，這件事我算了好幾遍，每次都一樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給很久沒聯絡的朋友，假裝很想念但其實什麼也沒說
variations: 一個禮拜沒見，換句話說，就是七天都沒碰到面。
我們上次見面是上次，這次見面是這次，中間隔的就是上次到這次。

en.content:
We haven't seen each other in three days,

which works out to roughly seventy-two hours.

And seventy-two hours, when you do the math,

comes out to exactly three days.

So it's been three days — I checked several times, and it's the same every time.
en.editorNote: Send this to a friend you haven't talked to in ages, pretending to miss them while saying nothing at all
variations: Not seeing you for a week means, in other words, seven whole days of not running into you.
Last time we met was last time, this time is this time, and the gap in between is from last time to this time.

items.sourceNote: 「待辦清單」觀察改寫為廢話文學

zh-tw.content:
你寫了一張待辦清單，

上面列了所有要做的事。

寫清單本身不在清單上，

但你做了。

於是你完成了一件清單上沒有的事，

而清單上有的事，一件都還沒開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 規劃了一整個下午卻什麼都沒做的時候，這句最能精準命中
variations: 整理書桌是為了讀書，結果整理完書桌就累了，書還在原地。
把事情寫下來的那一刻，你就有一種已經做完的錯覺。

en.content:
You made a to-do list

with everything you need to get done.

Making the list wasn't on the list,

but you did it anyway.

So you've completed a task that wasn't on the list,

while every task that is on the list remains untouched.
en.editorNote: Lands perfectly on the day you spent the whole afternoon planning and did nothing
variations: You tidy your desk so you can study, but after tidying you're too tired, and the book hasn't moved.
The moment you write something down, you get the illusion you've already done it.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「據我所知我一無所知」句式重新創作

zh-tw.content:
據我所知，

我所不知道的事，我都不知道。

而我知道的事，

我之所以知道，是因為我已經知道了。

所以我知道的就是我知道的，不知道的就是我不知道的，這點我非常清楚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被問到不會的問題時，用這句回答，對方會更困惑
variations: 我對這個領域的了解，僅限於我了解的那部分。
我能回答的問題，都是我會回答的問題。

en.content:
As far as I know,

everything I don't know, I don't know.

And the things I do know,

I know because I already know them.

So what I know is what I know, and what I don't know is what I don't know — on this I'm perfectly clear.
en.editorNote: Answer a question you have no clue about with this, and the asker will only get more confused
variations: My understanding of this field is limited to the part of it that I understand.
The questions I can answer are precisely the questions I'm able to answer.

items.sourceNote: 問答式廢話文學格式參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「你今天要去哪裡？」
「去我等一下會到的地方。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友追問你行程又不想講細節時，丟這句保證對方瞬間放棄
variations: 「你晚餐想吃什麼？」「吃我吃下去之後就會飽的東西。」
「你在看什麼？」「看我眼睛現在正對著的那個。」

en.content:
"Where are you headed today?"
"To the place I'll have arrived at later."
en.editorNote: Perfect for when a friend grills you about your plans and you'd rather not explain — they'll give up instantly
variations: "What do you want for dinner?" "Whatever fills me up once I've eaten it."
"What are you watching?" "The thing my eyes are currently pointed at."

items.sourceNote: technically-true 風格參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
理財專家提醒：當你把錢花掉之後，那筆錢就不再是你的了。
目前學界尚未找到例外。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在朋友亂買東西時一本正經地說出來，假裝給了很深的建議
variations: 存款的特性是：你存進去多少，帳戶裡就會多多少。這點科學家已反覆驗證。
錢包變薄通常代表裡面的東西變少了。

en.content:
Financial experts confirm that once you spend your money, that money is no longer yours.
No exception has been documented to date.
en.editorNote: Deliver this with a straight face when a friend is splurging — pretend you just gave priceless advice
variations: A key feature of savings: however much you put in, that's how much more your account holds. Repeatedly verified.
A thinner wallet usually means there's less inside it.

items.sourceNote: tautology / 同義反覆格式參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
只要你願意努力，你就是一個願意努力的人。
至於有沒有成果，那是另一個願意努力的問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在勵志場合假裝給結論，講完讓全場安靜三秒再爆笑
variations: 做得到的事情，通常都是你做得到的；做不到的，那就先放著。
成功的人之所以成功，是因為他們最後成功了。

en.content:
As long as you're willing to try, you are a person who is willing to try.
Whether it works out is a separate question about being willing to try.
en.editorNote: Great for faking a profound conclusion in a motivational moment — pause, then watch the room crack up
variations: The things you can do are usually the ones you can do; the rest can wait.
Successful people are successful because they ended up succeeding.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學六大手法參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
氣象局表示，今天會下雨的地方都會下雨，不會下雨的地方則維持不下雨。
請民眾依照所在地實際情況自行判斷。
zh-tw.editorNote: 出門前看到下雨可以傳給朋友，假裝幫他做了專業氣象分析
variations: 預報指出，明天的溫度會落在熱和冷之間的某個位置。
今天太陽出來的時候會比較亮，太陽下山之後則會變暗。

en.content:
The weather bureau reports that everywhere it rains today will get rain, while everywhere it doesn't will stay dry.
Residents are advised to assess their own local conditions.
en.editorNote: Text this to a friend before heading out in the rain, pretending you ran a pro weather analysis for them
variations: Forecasters note that tomorrow's temperature will land somewhere between hot and cold.
It'll be brighter today while the sun is up, and darker once it sets.

items.sourceNote: technically-true 笑話風格參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
睡眠研究顯示，你只要躺著閉上眼睛睡著，就能順利進入睡眠狀態。
醒著的人則普遍處於沒睡著的狀態。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的朋友傳訊息抱怨睡不著時，可以回這句假裝給專業建議
variations: 想要早起的關鍵，在於你前一天得先起得來才能再早起一次。
打哈欠通常是身體告訴你它剛剛打了一個哈欠。

en.content:
Sleep research shows that if you lie down, close your eyes, and fall asleep, you'll successfully enter a state of sleep.
People who are awake, meanwhile, are generally not asleep.
en.editorNote: When a sleepless friend texts to complain, reply with this and pretend it's expert guidance
variations: The key to waking up early is that you first have to wake up at all so you can do it again earlier.
A yawn is usually your body informing you that it just yawned.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「有道理」循環句型參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你說的這句話我覺得很對，對就對在它本身其實沒什麼對的地方，
但因為你覺得對、我也覺得對，於是它就慢慢地變成對的了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友認真跟你討論一件事、你又懶得反駁時，丟這句假裝深表認同
variations: 你問的這個問題問得非常好，好就好在它根本沒什麼好回答的。
這個想法我支持，支持的原因是它不需要任何原因就能被支持。

en.content:
What you just said strikes me as completely right — and it's right precisely because there's nothing actually right about it,
but since you think it's right and I think it's right, it slowly becomes right.
en.editorNote: When a friend earnestly explains something and you can't be bothered to argue, drop this to feign deep agreement
variations: That's a great question — great in the sense that there's nothing about it worth answering.
I fully support this idea, and the reason I support it is that it needs no reason to be supported.

items.sourceNote: technically-true 風格參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
理財專家指出，只要你花的錢比賺的少，剩下的那部分就會自動變成你的存款。
如果你花得比賺的多，這個現象就不會發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友抱怨存不到錢時，回這句假裝給你最頂級的理財建議
variations: 想要變有錢的第一步，是先確保你目前還沒有那麼有錢。
據統計，買得起的東西通常都在你的預算之內。

en.content:
Financial experts point out that as long as you spend less than you earn, whatever is left automatically becomes your savings.
If you spend more than you earn, this phenomenon does not occur.
en.editorNote: When a friend complains they can't save money, reply with this and pretend it's premium financial advice
variations: The first step to getting rich is making sure you aren't already that rich.
Studies show that things you can afford are usually within your budget.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「俗話說」自指句型參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
古人曾說過一句很有智慧的話，那句話的內容就是：古人曾說過一句很有智慧的話。
至於是哪句，至今仍是個謎。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想假裝引用名言但其實沒料時，這句最安全
variations: 俗話說得好，俗話說的就是會被人拿來說的那種話。
正所謂前人種樹，後人就會看到一棵被前人種好的樹。

en.content:
The ancients once spoke a deeply wise saying, and the saying went: the ancients once spoke a deeply wise saying.
Exactly which one remains a mystery to this day.
en.editorNote: Perfect for when you want to sound like you're quoting wisdom but have nothing in the tank
variations: As the old saying goes, the old saying is the kind of thing people say.
They say the early bird gets the worm — mostly because it was the one that showed up early.

items.sourceNote: technically-true 笑話風格參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
健身教練表示，只要你持續運動，身體就會處於正在運動的狀態。
一旦停下來，這個狀態便會立刻消失。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到朋友在群組立健身flag時，回這句假裝給專業指導
variations: 減重的關鍵在於你的體重要比現在還要再輕一點。
研究顯示，舉得起來的重量通常都在你舉得起來的範圍內。

en.content:
A personal trainer notes that as long as you keep exercising, your body will remain in a state of exercising.
The moment you stop, that state immediately disappears.
en.editorNote: When a friend posts a fitness resolution in the group chat, reply with this as if it's professional coaching
variations: The key to losing weight is for your weight to be a little lower than it currently is.
Research shows the weight you can lift is usually within the range of what you can lift.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學語錄參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
如果我沒有理解錯的話，那就代表我理解對了。
至於到底是哪一種，目前還在確認中。
zh-tw.editorNote: 開會時主管問你聽懂了沒，丟這句製造安全又模糊的回應
variations: 只要我沒搞錯，那這件事就沒被我搞錯。
我大概懂你的意思，前提是我真的懂。

en.content:
If I haven't misunderstood, that means I've understood correctly.
Which one it actually is remains under review.
en.editorNote: When your manager asks if you followed the meeting, reply with this for a safe yet meaningless answer
variations: As long as I didn't get it wrong, then I didn't get it wrong.
I basically get what you mean, assuming I actually get it.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學喝水長壽風格，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
醫學統計指出，凡是每天喝水的人，最後幾乎都離開了這個世界。
至今尚未發現例外。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友在群組分享養生文時，回這句假裝補充專業數據
variations: 數據顯示，吃早餐的人壽命有限，不吃的人也一樣。
規律睡覺的人，醒來的機率非常高。

en.content:
Medical statistics show that nearly everyone who drinks water every day eventually leaves this world.
No exception has been recorded so far.
en.editorNote: When a friend shares wellness tips in the chat, drop this as if adding expert data
variations: Data shows people who eat breakfast have a finite lifespan — and so do those who skip it.
People who sleep regularly have an extremely high chance of waking up.

items.sourceNote: technically-true 笑話風格參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
什麼東西又綠又會跳？
如果風夠大，那就是草。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想冷場時丟出來的萬用謎語，保證對話瞬間凝固
variations: 什麼東西是紅色又聞起來像紅油漆？答案是紅油漆。
為什麼叫小山？因為它是一座比較小的山。

en.content:
What's green and jumps?
Grass — if the wind is strong enough.
en.editorNote: A go-to riddle for killing the mood; guaranteed to freeze any conversation
variations: What's red and smells like red paint? Red paint.
Why do they call it a small mountain? Because it's a smaller mountain.

items.sourceNote: technically-true 占卜笑話風格，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我今天的星座運勢寫著：「將會有事情發生。」
到了晚上回頭看，它說得一點都沒錯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友太迷信星座運勢時，傳這句溫柔吐槽
variations: 算命師說我未來會遇到一個人，結果我每天都遇到很多人，太準了。
預言說明年會過去，目前看來相當可信。

en.content:
My horoscope today read: "Something will happen."
Looking back tonight, it turned out to be completely accurate.
en.editorNote: Send this to gently roast a friend who takes their horoscope too seriously
variations: A fortune teller said I'd meet someone — and I met dozens of people that day. Spot on.
The prophecy says next year will pass. So far it's holding up remarkably well.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學自指句型參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
如果我沒有講錯的話，那這句話就是對的。
如果我講錯了，那這句話就不是這句話了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在群組裡丟出來，看大家認真思考三秒後決定不理你
variations: 據我所知，我其實不太知道。但我知道我不知道，這點我很確定。
這句話如果是真的，那它就是真的；如果是假的，那它就不是這句話。

en.content:
If I'm not mistaken, then this sentence is correct.
If I am mistaken, then this sentence is no longer this sentence.
en.editorNote: Perfect for dropping in a chat and watching everyone think hard for three seconds before deciding to ignore you
variations: As far as I know, I don't really know much. But I do know that I don't know — that part I'm sure of.
If this sentence is true, then it's true; if it's false, then it isn't this sentence.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學時間循環例句，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
只要把這個禮拜撐過去，下個禮拜就會自動到來。
而下個禮拜撐過去之後，再下個禮拜也會到。
人生就是這樣一個禮拜接一個禮拜過去的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一上班前傳給同事，給彼此一點虛無的力量
variations: 只要熬過今天，明天就到了；熬過明天，後天也會到。日子就是這樣一天天被熬過去的。
等到月底，這個月就結束了。這是經過長期觀察得出的結論。

en.content:
As long as you get through this week, next week will arrive on its own.
And once you get through next week, the week after will arrive too.
That's how life goes — one week after another.
en.editorNote: Send this to a coworker before Monday for a little dose of motivational nihilism
variations: Just survive today and tomorrow shows up; survive tomorrow and the next day shows up too. That's how the days get crossed off.
Wait until the end of the month and the month will be over. This conclusion is based on extensive observation.

items.sourceNote: technically-true 拼字梗，重新創作改寫

zh-tw.content:
「錯字」這兩個字唯一不會是錯字的時候，就是當它真的指一個錯字的時候。
其他時候它都對得不能再對。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都要抓你錯字的那位朋友，以毒攻毒
variations: 把字打對，這件事本身永遠不會打錯，除非你把「對」打成「錯」。
「簡短」這兩個字其實不算短，這點有點諷刺。

en.content:
The only time the word "incorrectly" is spelled incorrectly is when it's actually spelled incorrectly.
Every other time, it's about as correct as a word can get.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who always corrects your typos — fight fire with fire
variations: The word "misspelled" is hardly ever misspelled, which is honestly its biggest flex.
The word "short" is, ironically, not that short to type out.

items.sourceNote: technically-true 一次性事實梗，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
你可以把水泥喝下去，只是這件事你一輩子只能做一次。
做完之後，你大概也不太需要再做第二次了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在朋友說「這個應該可以吧」的時候冷冷補一句
variations: 每個人都能跳傘，差別只在於你想不想用第二次。
理論上你可以吃下任何東西，能不能吃第二口才是重點。

en.content:
You can drink cement — it's just something you only get to do once.
After that, you probably won't feel the need to do it again.
en.editorNote: Pull this out the moment a friend says "that should be fine, right?"
variations: Everyone can skydive. The only question is whether you'd want a second go.
Technically you can eat anything. Whether you get a second bite is the real question.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學重複句型參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你現在正在讀這句話。
讀完之後，你就讀完了這句話。
至於讀到了什麼，那是另一個問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放在貼文最上面，騙人看完整篇結果什麼都沒有
variations: 聽完這段話，你會發現你聽完了這段話。
看完這本書的人，通常都已經把這本書看完了。

en.content:
You are currently reading this sentence.
Once you finish, you will have finished reading this sentence.
What you got out of it is a separate matter.
en.editorNote: Great as the opening line of a post — lures people into reading the whole thing for nothing
variations: After hearing this, you'll realize you've heard this.
People who finish this book have usually, by then, finished the book.

items.sourceNote: 同義反覆笑話句型參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
廢話社的第一條規則，就是廢話社的第一條規則。
至於第二條，我們開會討論過了，結論是還沒討論。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在群組裡有人問「規則是什麼」的時候丟出去，保證對話直接陷入沉默
variations: 想加入廢話社，首先你要先想加入廢話社。
廢話社不對外公開規則，因為公開了就不叫不公開了。

en.content:
The first rule of the Nonsense Club is the first rule of the Nonsense Club.
As for the second rule, we held a meeting about it — and concluded we hadn't discussed it yet.
en.editorNote: Use this when someone asks 'what are the rules?' in a group chat — guaranteed to kill the conversation instantly
variations: To join the Nonsense Club, the first step is wanting to join the Nonsense Club.
The Nonsense Club keeps its rules private, because once they're public they're no longer private.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「先別急」句型參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我知道你很急，但你先別急。
等你不急了，這件事大概也急不起來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友熱鍋上螞蟻時丟給他，安慰效果為零，但至少能讓他笑出來
variations: 你越急它越不會好，等它好了你也不用急了。
急也是一天，不急也是一天，差別是急的那天比較累。

en.content:
I know you're in a hurry, but don't be in a hurry.
By the time you stop hurrying, there'll probably be nothing left to hurry about.
en.editorNote: Send this to a friend who's freaking out — zero comfort value, but it'll at least make them laugh
variations: The more you rush it, the less it works; and when it finally works, there's no need to rush.
A rushed day is still a day; an unrushed day is also a day. The only difference is the rushed one is more exhausting.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「出意外」句型參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
如果一切順利的話，那就是一切順利。
如果不順利的話，那大概就是出意外了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在工作群組報告進度時用，主管看了會很想揍你
variations: 計畫進行得很好，唯一的問題是還沒開始。
只要不出意外，就不會出意外，問題是意外通常不打招呼。

en.content:
If everything goes smoothly, then everything goes smoothly.
If it doesn't, then something unexpected has probably happened.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a status update in a work chat — your manager will want to throw something at you
variations: The plan is going great; the only issue is it hasn't started.
As long as nothing unexpected happens, nothing unexpected happens — the catch is that the unexpected rarely warns you first.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「開玩笑」反轉句型參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我爸有三棟房，我媽有兩台車，我哥有一間公司。
而我，有他們這麼厲害的家人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在朋友炫耀家境時接話，把對話導向溫馨又廢的方向
variations: 我家開三間餐廳、兩家飯店、一座工廠，我負責去吃、去住、去參觀。
我們全家都很有才華，爸會做生意、媽會理財、我會欣賞他們。

en.content:
My dad owns three houses, my mom owns two cars, my brother owns a company.
And me — I own the privilege of being related to them.
en.editorNote: Use this to one-up a friend who's bragging about their family — steers the convo somewhere warm and pointless
variations: My family runs three restaurants, two hotels, and a factory; my job is to eat, stay over, and take the tour.
Everyone in my family is gifted — Dad does business, Mom handles money, and I appreciate them.

items.sourceNote: 「Wherever you go, there you are」同義反覆名句參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
無論你走到哪裡，你都在那裡。
這也是為什麼你永遠甩不掉自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在出去玩的限動配風景照，看起來很有意境，其實什麼都沒說
variations: 不管你飛多遠，下飛機的還是你。
換個城市生活，唯一沒換掉的就是自己。

en.content:
Wherever you go, there you are.
Which is also why you can never quite escape yourself.
en.editorNote: Pair it with a scenic photo on your travel story — looks deep, says nothing
variations: No matter how far you fly, the person getting off the plane is still you.
You can move to a new city, but the one thing you can't swap out is yourself.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學自我重複句型，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
如果我沒講錯的話，那我應該就是沒講錯。
當然，這個前提是我真的沒講錯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在被問到沒把握的事情時，自信地回這句，對方會愣三秒
variations: 我可以很肯定地跟你說，我並不是很肯定。
這件事我覺得對，前提是它真的對。

en.content:
If I'm not mistaken, then I am, in fact, not mistaken.
Assuming, of course, that I'm not mistaken.
en.editorNote: Say this with full confidence when someone asks you something you're unsure about — watch them freeze for three seconds
variations: I can say with absolute certainty that I'm not at all certain.
I'm pretty sure this is right, provided it actually is right.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學貧窮主題句，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
你知道我為什麼一直存不到錢嗎？
因為我的錢一直沒有變多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在月底發薪前傳給一樣窮的朋友互相取暖
variations: 我不是亂花錢，我只是花完之後錢就沒了。
想變有錢的第一步，就是先有錢。

en.content:
You know why I can never save any money?
Because the amount of money I have keeps not increasing.
en.editorNote: Send this to your equally broke friend right before payday for some mutual comfort
variations: I don't waste money — I just spend it, and then it's gone.
Step one to getting rich is to first have money.

items.sourceNote: tautology / lapalissade 句型，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
醫生說，等到不痛的時候，就會比較不痛了。
我聽完覺得很有道理，但好像哪裡怪怪的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在朋友抱怨身體不舒服時，一本正經地安慰他
variations: 別擔心，傷口癒合之後就不會是傷口了。
撐過最累的時候，剩下的就不是最累的時候了。

en.content:
The doctor told me it'll feel better once it stops hurting.
I nodded, and only later realized something was off.
en.editorNote: Use it to 'comfort' a friend complaining about an ache — deliver it with a totally straight face
variations: Don't worry, once the wound heals it won't be a wound anymore.
Get through the hardest part and the rest won't be the hardest part.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學時間循環句型，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
經過研究發現，只要熬過了今天，明天就會到來。
而明天熬過去之後，又會變成今天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在加班到深夜或熬夜趕報告時，傳給一起苦的同事
variations: 再撐一下，因為再不撐一下就撐不下去了。
週一最難的地方，就是它總是接在週日後面。

en.content:
Studies show that if you just survive today, tomorrow will arrive.
And once you survive tomorrow, it becomes today again.
en.editorNote: Send it to a coworker grinding through a late night or an all-nighter deadline
variations: Just hang on a little longer, because if you don't, you won't be hanging on.
The hardest part about Monday is that it always comes right after Sunday.
